handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:286:1. (**Codex R10#1 — substantive**) `_smoke_siliconflow.py` doesn't fail closed on missing keys: the script skipped unset secondary/tertiary and returned PASS if any configured key responded. The packet's "PASS (3/3 keys)" claim was strictly stronger than what the smoke verified — a primary-only setup would silently PASS, replicating exactly the V3L-27 single-key collapse pattern the 3-key pool was meant to mitigate.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:287:2. (**Codex R10#2 — substantive**) Audit runner scripts (`run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh` + `run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py`) are load-bearing gate tools (demonstrated by R8/R9's runner-default + false-closure defects) but were NOT in Trust Root. Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification): tools producing data used by future dual audit must enter Trust Root.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:289:**Round-10 fixes shipped (`A8e11`, this commit)** — two fixes (both substantive):
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:299:- Test baseline: 265 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed (Rust); 16/16 PASS (Python)
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:300:- Trust Root: 37-entry manifest
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:304:- Gemini R11: **CHALLENGE / high** — `handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md`. *First time both auditors converged on multiple substantive findings since R7.*
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:305:- Merged: **CHALLENGE**. Two substantive findings (one architectural, one refactor) plus four documentary.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:308:1. (**Codex R11#1 — substantive architectural**) Trust Root proxy via `cases/MANIFEST.sha256` + `rules/MANIFEST.sha256` is documented as covering the child yaml files but not actually enforced at boot. `verify_trust_root` only hashes the flat `[trust_root]` entries; the manifest's child contents are not verified. A direct edit to a case or rule yaml without regenerating its parent manifest leaves boot still passing — silent governance drift.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:311:4. (Codex R11#4) `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` cumulative metrics paragraph had a contradiction: claimed "11 substantive findings" but the enumerated list ended with "= 9 real bugs".
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:314:7. (**Gemini R11#3 — substantive refactor**) `make_pput` function signature uses `Option<u64>` for `total_run_token_count` / `failed_branch_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`, but every caller passes `Some(...)` and the body unwraps with `.unwrap_or(0)`. The mid-term P0-B fix collapsed the v2 struct fields to non-Option but didn't follow through to the function-parameter contract. Code clarity issue, not a runtime bug.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:316:**Round-11 fixes shipped (`A8e13`, this commit)** — six fixes (2 substantive + 4 documentary):
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:317:- **Q1** (Codex R11#1 — substantive arch): `src/boot.rs` extended with `verify_child_manifest`. When a Trust Root entry path ends in `/MANIFEST.sha256`, the verifier now ALSO parses the manifest contents (GNU sha256sum format) and verifies each child file's actual hash against the claim. Manifests regenerated with repo-relative paths (was a mix of bare-filename and dir-relative). Two new unit tests pin the contract (`verify_trust_root_detects_child_manifest_tamper` + `verify_trust_root_passes_with_matching_child_manifest`). Plus: `src/boot.rs` itself added to Trust Root (meta-finding caught during fix — the verifier file was not previously protected by the verifier; tampering with boot.rs would silently bypass the entire gate). Manifest 37 → 38.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:318:- **Q2** (Gemini R11#3 — substantive refactor): `make_pput` signature `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for the 3 affected parameters. All 9 production call sites + 4 test fixtures updated. `wc.elapsed_ms()` returns `Option<u64>` upstream; call sites add `.unwrap_or(0)` at the boundary. `cargo test --workspace`: 267 PASS (was 265 + 2 new boot tests).
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:322:- **Q6** (Codex R11#4): cumulative metrics paragraph reworded — "11 substantive findings" enumeration extended to 13 (was missing R10's P1+P2 = 2 findings; now lists all explicitly).
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:346:- Gemini R12: **CHALLENGE / high** — `handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md`. *Independent run_swarm review surfaced one new substantive bug.*
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:347:- Merged: **CHALLENGE**. One substantive run_swarm behavior bug (Gemini) + cascade of stale 37/265 documentary references after A8e13 (Codex).
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:350:1. (Codex R12#1) Packet still reports 37-entry manifest + 265 PASS (was current at A8e12; A8e13 bumped both to 38 / 267 but didn't propagate to the packet's metric table at § 2, § 4 checklist, § 6 Q4.a question).
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:351:2. (Codex R12#2) Audit runner script header `printf` blocks still emit "265 PASS + 37-entry manifest" — Trust-Rooted gate machinery emitting stale baselines into every R12+ transcript.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:353:4. (**Gemini R12 — substantive run_swarm bug**) When an agent's `search_count >= search_cap` (default 20), the search tool is correctly stripped from `tools_desc` (line 870), but `search_cache[agent_id]` is not cleared. The cached results from the agent's last successful search keep being injected into every subsequent prompt's `hits_ref` (line 869) for the rest of the run. The agent reasons from stale data forever; uncontrolled experimental variable that would confound any swarm experiment that exercises the cap.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:355:**Round-12 fixes shipped (`A8e14`, this commit)** — two threads:
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:357:- **R2 (Gemini R12 — substantive)**: `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:869–885` reworked to gate both `hits_ref` AND `tools_desc` through a single `cap_hit` boolean. When `cap_hit` is true, the prompt receives `hits_ref = Vec::new()` (was the cached old hits). Single source of truth; can't drift. FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI prompt construction).
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:373:| 9 | CHALLENGE | **PASS** | CHALLENGE | 2 | 0 (false-closure caught — N3 was incomplete; no new substantive bugs) | ~5 |
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:377:| 13 | pending | pending | pending | — | — | ~5 |
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:379:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.

   254	        // Generic nN: parse any "n<digits>" → run_swarm with N agents.
   255	        // Supports N-scaling experiment (percolation curve mapping).
   256	        // **swarm_N=1** (CONDITION=n1) is the critical baseline for the
   257	        // 2026-04-25 N-experiments arc: same code path as n3/n8 swarm
   258	        // but with a single agent. NOT the same as `oneshot` (which
   259	        // skips the swarm loop, tape, mr ticks, ∏p product, etc.).
   260	        // Per Plan-agent NEXT-1 / Codex E0 / Gemini E1-Prime: every
   261	        // N-curve experiment MUST use n1 (not oneshot) as the N=1
   262	        // baseline to avoid code-path confound. Validated by unit
   263	        // test below: parse_swarm_condition_n("n1") == Some(1).
   264	        c if parse_swarm_condition_n(c).is_some() => {
   265	            let n = parse_swarm_condition_n(c).unwrap();
   266	            run_swarm(problem_file, &problem_statement, &theorem_name,
   267	                     &lean_path, &proxy_url, &model, n).await
   268	        }
   269	        "hybrid_v1" => {
   270	            // Mid-term audit P0-D fix 2026-04-25: hybrid_v1 was a Paper 1 era
   271	            // condition that ran run_oneshot, then on failure ran run_swarm,
   272	            // and merged via `..r2` field-spread. Codex flagged that the spread
   273	            // dropped the failed oneshot's C_i (failed_branch_count and
   274	            // total_run_token_count from r1 were silently discarded). PPUT-CCL
   275	            // arc does NOT use hybrid_v1 — it operates exclusively on `oneshot`
   276	            // and `n<N>` conditions per PREREG. Disabling here forces any
   277	            // pipeline that ships a stale hybrid_v1 invocation to surface the
   278	            // deprecation immediately rather than emit a corrupt C_i.
   279	            eprintln!("hybrid_v1 condition is deprecated for PPUT-CCL arc and was \
   280	                       disabled in mid-term audit P0-D fix 2026-04-25. The prior \
   281	                       implementation dropped the failed oneshot leg's C_i via a \
   282	                       `..r2` field-spread, corrupting full-run cost accounting. \
   283	                       Use `oneshot` or `n<N>` instead.");
   284	            std::process::exit(1);
   285	        }
   286	        other => { eprintln!("Unknown condition: {}", other); std::process::exit(1); }
   287	    };
   288	
   289	    // Output PPUT result as JSON (machine-readable for batch runner)
   290	    let json = serde_json::to_string(&result).unwrap();
   291	    println!("PPUT_RESULT:{}", json);
   292	
   293	    if result.has_golden_path {
   294	        info!("PPUT = {:.2}%/s (GP: {} nodes, {} tokens, {:.1}s)",
   295	              result.pput, result.gp_node_count, result.gp_token_count, result.time_secs);
   296	    } else {
   297	        info!("PPUT = 0 (no golden path in {:.1}s, {} tx)", result.time_secs, result.tx_count);
   298	    }
   299	}
   300	
   301	fn resolve_problem_path(problem_file: &str, minif2f_dir: &str) -> String {
   302	    if PathBuf::from(problem_file).exists() {
   303	        return problem_file.to_string();
   304	    }
   305	    let test_path = format!("{}/MiniF2F/Test/{}", minif2f_dir, problem_file);
   306	    if PathBuf::from(&test_path).exists() { return test_path; }
   307	    let valid_path = format!("{}/MiniF2F/Valid/{}", minif2f_dir, problem_file);
   308	    if PathBuf::from(&valid_path).exists() { return valid_path; }
   309	    eprintln!("Problem file not found: {}", problem_file);
   310	    std::process::exit(1);
   311	}
   312	
   313	/// Oneshot: single LLM call → verify → PPUT.
   314	async fn run_oneshot(
   315	    problem_file: &str, problem_statement: &str, theorem_name: &str,
   316	    lean_path: &str, proxy_url: &str, model: &str,
   317	) -> PputResult {
   318	    let start = Instant::now();
   319	    let mut acc = RunCostAccumulator::new();
   320	    let mut wc = RunWallClock::new();
   321	    // Phase A atom A4 (FC1-N12 oracle scope): cumulative wall-clock
   322	    // inside Lean for this oneshot run. A single verify_omega call,
   323	    // but bracket so future Phase C Soft Law mode that double-verifies
   324	    // accumulates correctly.
   325	    let mut verifier_wait_ms: u64 = 0;
   326	    // Phase A atom A5 (FC2-N22 budget regime stamp): oneshot has no
   327	    // transaction loop — it issues exactly one LLM call and returns.
   328	    // Stamp `total_proposal` + base=1 so downstream PPUT analysis can
   329	    // join oneshot rows on the same regime axis as swarm rows without
   330	    // a special case. The regime is informational here; no scaling.
   331	    let oneshot_regime = minif2f_v4::budget_regime::BudgetRegime::TotalProposal;
   332	    let oneshot_budget_base: u32 = 1;
   333	
   334	    // A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4): one run_id minted at function
   335	    // entry, passed to both fc_event!s and make_pput. Eliminates the
   336	    // millisecond drift between `run_corr_id` (was generated here) and
   337	    // make_pput's internal recomputation. Phase D consumers can now
   338	    // join FC events to v2 jsonl rows by `run_id` equality.
   339	    let run_id = minif2f_v4::run_id::mint_run_id("oneshot", problem_file);
   340	
   341	    let oracle = Lean4Oracle::new(
   342	        problem_statement.to_string(), theorem_name.to_string(), lean_path.to_string(),
   343	    );
   344	
   345	    // PPUT-CCL B3 (mid-term audit P0-C fix 2026-04-25): open the wall-clock
   346	    // bracket BEFORE prompt construction. PREREG § 5 / plan B3 define T_i
   347	    // as "first agent prompt construction → final Lean call". Marking after
   348	    // the construction (prior wiring) under-counted prompt-build time and
   349	    // forced the conformance test to relax its 7100ms assertion.
   350	    wc.mark_first_read();
   351	
   352	    // R-22 v2 clause 4 stays reject-only; the prompt must prevent fences at the source.
   353	    // Chat models (deepseek-chat, 2026-04-22) default to ```lean fences; verifier hard-rejects
   354	    // any response containing ``` so the instruction must be explicit. See F-2026-04-22-08.
   355	    let prompt = format!(
   356	        "Complete the following Lean 4 proof. Output ONLY the tactic proof body as raw Lean \
   357	         tokens. DO NOT wrap in markdown code fences (no ```). No prose, no backticks.\n\n{}",
   358	        problem_statement
   359	    );
   360	
   361	    let client = ResilientLLMClient::new(proxy_url, 1800, 2);
   362	    // Model-aware max_tokens: deepseek-chat caps at 8192; reasoner needs 16000 for thinking.
   363	    let max_toks = if model.contains("chat") { 8000 } else { 16000 };
   364	    let request = GenerateRequest {
   365	        model: model.to_string(),
   366	        messages: vec![Message { role: "user".into(), content: prompt }],
   367	        temperature: Some(0.2),
   368	        max_tokens: Some(max_toks),
   369	    };
   370	
   371	    // PPUT-CCL B6 runtime gate: scan the assembled prompt for PPUT scalars
   372	    // before the call goes out. Any leak aborts deterministically — Goodhart
   373	    // shield at the LLM-call boundary.
   374	    assert_no_metric_leak(&request.messages[0].content);
   375	    match client.generate(&request).await {
   376	        Ok(response) => {
   377	            acc.record_llm_call(response.prompt_tokens, response.completion_tokens);
   378	            acc.record_proposal(false);
   379	            // Rule 22 v2 clause 4: reject markdown fences
   380	            if response.content.contains("```") {
   381	                wc.mark_final_accept();
   382	                // P0-A: caller declares both runtime + post-hoc legs.
   383	                // Fence reject = neither leg fired.
   384	                // A4: no Lean call reached → verifier_wait_ms=0;
   385	                // 1 proposal made (the LLM response), 1 distinct.
   386	                return make_pput(problem_file, "oneshot", model,
   387	                                 false, false, start, 0, 0, 1,
   388	                                 None, None, None, None, None,
   389	                                 acc.total_run_token_count(),
   390	                                 acc.failed_branch_count,
   391	                                 wc.elapsed_ms().unwrap_or(0),
   392	                                 false, 1, 1, verifier_wait_ms,
   393	                                 oneshot_regime, oneshot_budget_base, &run_id);
   394	            }
   395

   476	        Err(e) => {
   477	            // C-012: measurement failure ≠ verified failure.
   478	            // Do not emit PPUT_RESULT — batch runner must retry on resume.
   479	            // C-017: broadcast error explicitly (stderr, non-zero exit).
   480	            error!("LLM error: {}", e);
   481	            eprintln!("MEASUREMENT_ERROR oneshot LLM: {}", e);
   482	            std::process::exit(2);
   483	        }
   484	    }
   485	}
   486	
   487	/// Swarm: N agents, prediction market, Boltzmann routing → PPUT.
   488	async fn run_swarm(
   489	    problem_file: &str, problem_statement: &str, theorem_name: &str,
   490	    lean_path: &str, proxy_url: &str, model: &str, n_agents: usize,
   491	) -> PputResult {
   492	    let start = Instant::now();
   493	    let condition = format!("n{}", n_agents);
   494	
   495	    // A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4): single run_id minted ONCE per
   496	    // run, threaded into both fc_event!s and make_pput. Replaces the
   497	    // round-1 `run_corr_id` (FC events) ↔ make_pput-internal `run_id`
   498	    // (v2 jsonl) split that introduced millisecond drift on the join key.
   499	    let run_id = minif2f_v4::run_id::mint_run_id(&condition, problem_file);
   500	
   501	    let kernel = Kernel::new();
   502	    let config = BusConfig {
   503	        // Phase 2.1 (C-043 candidate): OMEGA-accepted proofs are auto-written
   504	        // as tape nodes (mandatory wtool per Art. IV). Full proofs can be
   505	        // long; raise bus caps so winning nodes don't get size-vetoed. Agent
   506	        // partials still typically <1200; no behavioural regression.
   507	        max_payload_chars: 8000,
   508	        max_payload_lines: 200,
   509	        system_lp_amount: 200.0,
   510	        // C-011: decide/omega/native_decide forbidden (brute-force precedent)
   511	        forbidden_patterns: vec![
   512	            "native_decide".into(), "decide".into(), "omega".into(),
   513	            "#eval".into(), "IO.Process".into(),
   514	            "IO.FS".into(), "run_tac".into(), "unsafe".into(),
   515	        ],
   516	    };
   517	
   518	    // Phase 1: opt-in tape persistence via env. WAL_DIR=<dir> enables WAL
   519	    // writes to <dir>/<problem>_<timestamp>.jsonl; resumes if file exists.
   520	    // Default off for backward-compat baseline runs.
   521	    let mut bus = if let Ok(wal_dir) = std::env::var("WAL_DIR") {
   522	        let problem_stem = std::path::Path::new(problem_file)
   523	            .file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
   524	            .unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown".into());
   525	        let resume_id = std::env::var("WAL_RESUME_ID").ok();
   526	        let id = resume_id.unwrap_or_else(|| {
   527	            std::time::SystemTime::now()
   528	                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   529	                .map(|d| d.as_secs().to_string())
   530	                .unwrap_or_else(|_| "0".into())
   531	        });
   532	        let wal_path = std::path::Path::new(&wal_dir)
   533	            .join(format!("{}_{}.jsonl", problem_stem, id));
   534	        info!("[wal] using {:?}", wal_path);
   535	        match TuringBus::with_wal_path(kernel, config, wal_path) {
   536	            Ok(b) => b,
   537	            Err(e) => {
   538	                error!("[wal] open failed: {} — falling back to in-memory", e);
   539	                TuringBus::new(Kernel::new(), BusConfig {
   540	                    max_payload_chars: 1200, max_payload_lines: 18,
   541	                    system_lp_amount: 200.0,
   542	                    forbidden_patterns: vec![
   543	                        "native_decide".into(), "decide".into(), "omega".into(),
   544	                        "#eval".into(), "IO.Process".into(), "IO.FS".into(),
   545	                        "run_tac".into(), "unsafe".into(),
   546	                    ],
   547	                })
   548	            }
   549	        }
   550	    } else {
   551	        TuringBus::new(kernel, config)
   552	    };
   553	    // Phase 4 (C-041 candidate): cross-problem wallet persistence. WALLET_STATE
   554	    // env points to a json file; if it exists we load agents' carried-over
   555	    // balances/portfolios, otherwise fresh genesis. No second mint under Law 2:
   556	    // genesis_done is serialised, so on_init is a no-op post first boot.
   557	    let wallet_state_path: Option<std::path::PathBuf> = std::env::var("WALLET_STATE")
   558	        .ok().map(std::path::PathBuf::from);
   559	    let wallet = wallet_state_path.as_ref()
   560	        .and_then(|p| WalletTool::load_from_disk(p))
   561	        .unwrap_or_else(|| WalletTool::new(10000.0));
   562	    if wallet_state_path.is_some() && wallet.genesis_done {
   563	        info!("[wallet] resumed from {:?}; existing agents carry balances",
   564	              wallet_state_path);
   565	    }
   566	    bus.mount_tool(Box::new(wallet));
   567	    bus.mount_tool(Box::new(Lean4Oracle::new(
   568	        problem_statement.to_string(), theorem_name.to_string(), lean_path.to_string(),
   569	    )));
   570	    bus.mount_tool(Box::new(SearchTool::new(
   571	        vec![format!("{}/MiniF2F/Test", std::env::var("MINIF2F_DIR")
   572	            .unwrap_or_else(|_| DEFAULT_MINIF2F_DIR.into()))], 20,
   573	    )));
   574	    bus.mount_tool(Box::new(LibrarianTool::new(
   575	        &format!("{}/skills", std::env::var("EXPERIMENT_DIR").unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".into())), 8,
   576	    )));
   577	
   578	    let agent_ids: Vec<String> = (0..n_agents).map(|i| format!("Agent_{}", i)).collect();
   579	    bus.init(&agent_ids);
   580	    // Phase 4: top-up ensure_agents for any IDs not in the loaded state (zero
   581	    // balance if post-genesis, genesis_coins only on first-ever boot).
   582	    if let Some(wallet) = bus.tools.iter_mut()
   583	        .find_map(|t| t.as_any_mut().downcast_mut::<WalletTool>())
   584	    {
   585	        wallet.ensure_agents(&agent_ids);

  1493	
  1494	fn make_pput(
  1495	    problem: &str, condition: &str, model: &str,
  1496	    runtime_accepted: bool, post_hoc_verified: bool, start: Instant,
  1497	    gp_tokens: u64, gp_nodes: usize, tx_count: u64,
  1498	    tool_dist: Option<HashMap<String, u32>>,
  1499	    unique_payload_ratio: Option<f64>,
  1500	    gp_payload: Option<String>,
  1501	    gp_path: Option<String>,
  1502	    gp_proof_file: Option<String>,
  1503	    total_run_token_count: u64,
  1504	    failed_branch_count: u32,
  1505	    total_wall_time_ms: u64,
  1506	    // Phase A atom A4 (decomposed metrics). All callers must pass
  1507	    // explicit values — the v2 fields are non-Optional.
  1508	    hit_max_tx: bool,
  1509	    distinct_proposals: u64,
  1510	    total_proposals: u64,
  1511	    verifier_wait_ms: u64,
  1512	    // Phase A atom A5 (FC2-N22 budget regime stamp). Caller declares
  1513	    // the regime + base BEFORE the loop so MaxTxExhausted rows are
  1514	    // unambiguous about which partitioning rule produced them.
  1515	    budget_regime: minif2f_v4::budget_regime::BudgetRegime,
  1516	    budget_max_transactions: u32,
  1517	    // A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4): run_id minted by caller (run_swarm
  1518	    // or run_oneshot) at function entry; passed in here so the v2 jsonl
  1519	    // row stamps the SAME identifier the FC events used. No more ms drift.
  1520	    run_id: &str,
  1521	) -> PputResult {
  1522	    // PPUT-CCL Phase B B4 (mid-term audit P0-A fix 2026-04-25):
  1523	    // make_pput is now PURELY computational. The caller MUST decide both
  1524	    // `runtime_accepted` (did the evaluator's runtime gate fire?) and
  1525	    // `post_hoc_verified` (did Lean independently confirm the proof?). The
  1526	    // prior implementation derived `post_hoc_verified = has_gp` internally,
  1527	    // which would have laundered Phase C Soft Law fake-accepts into the
  1528	    // North Star pput_verified. Forcing the caller to pass both legs makes
  1529	    // Soft Law's design point unmissable: any caller that fakes runtime
  1530	    // accept must explicitly pass post_hoc_verified=verify_post_hoc(...)
  1531	    // or the divergence will surface immediately.
  1532	    //
  1533	    // Phase B all callers pass `(runtime_accepted, post_hoc_verified) = (X, X)`
  1534	    // because runtime IS Lean today. Phase C diverges at the Soft Law
  1535	    // mode call site, not inside this function.
  1536	    let has_gp = runtime_accepted; // legacy `has_golden_path` field semantics
  1537	    let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
  1538	    let pput = if has_gp && elapsed > 0.0 { 100.0 / elapsed } else { 0.0 };
  1539	    // C-012 provenance: populated from env vars; None when unset (backward compat).
  1540	    let build_sha = std::env::var("BUILD_SHA").ok();
  1541	    let classifier_version = std::env::var("CLASSIFIER_VERSION").ok();
  1542	    let boltzmann_seed = std::env::var("BOLTZMANN_SEED")
  1543	        .ok().and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
  1544	
  1545	    // PREREG § 5 constitutional notation: C_i (full-run cost) + T_i (wall clock).
  1546	    let c_i = total_run_token_count;
  1547	    let t_i = total_wall_time_ms;
  1548	
  1549	    let progress_runtime = compute_progress_runtime(runtime_accepted);
  1550	    let progress_verified =
  1551	        compute_progress_verified(runtime_accepted, post_hoc_verified);
  1552	    let pput_runtime = compute_pput(progress_runtime, c_i, t_i);
  1553	    let pput_verified = compute_pput(progress_verified, c_i, t_i);
  1554	    let pput_m_verified = compute_pput_m(progress_verified, c_i, t_i);
  1555	
  1556	    // V2 fields read from env (per-process globals).
  1557	    let split = std::env::var("SPLIT").unwrap_or_else(|_| {
  1558	        eprintln!("[v2-emit] SPLIT env unset; defaulting to 'adaptation' \
  1559	                   (Phase B convention; pre-registration requires SPLIT \
  1560	                   for Phase C+ ablation runs)");
  1561	        "adaptation".to_string()
  1562	    });
  1563	    let mode = std::env::var("MODE").unwrap_or_else(|_| "full".to_string());
  1564	    let model_snapshot = std::env::var("MODEL_SNAPSHOT")
  1565	        .unwrap_or_else(|_| model.to_string());
  1566	    let git_sha = build_sha.clone().unwrap_or_default();
  1567	    let binary_sha256 = std::env::var("BINARY_SHA256").unwrap_or_default();
  1568	
  1569	    // problem_id = basename without .lean
  1570	    let problem_id = std::path::Path::new(problem)
  1571	        .file_stem()
  1572	        .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
  1573	        .unwrap_or(problem)
  1574	        .to_string();
  1575	
  1576	    PputResult {
  1577	        // ── B1 v2 schema fields ──
  1578	        schema_version: "v2.0".to_string(),
  1579	        // A8e fix F1: caller-supplied run_id (matches the FC-trace
  1580	        // correlation key emitted at every fc_event! site). No more
  1581	        // ms drift between the two identifiers.
  1582	        run_id: run_id.to_string(),
  1583	        problem_id,
  1584	        solved: runtime_accepted,
  1585	        split,

   119	# ── Token Metering ──
   120	_stats_lock = threading.Lock()
   121	_stats = {
   122	    "prompt_tokens": 0,
   123	    "completion_tokens": 0,
   124	    "total_tokens": 0,
   125	    "requests": 0,
   126	    "errors": 0,
   127	    "retries_429": 0,
   128	    "estimated_count": 0,
   129	}
   130	
   131	
   132	def _record_usage(prompt_tokens, completion_tokens):
   133	    with _stats_lock:
   134	        _stats["prompt_tokens"] += prompt_tokens
   135	        _stats["completion_tokens"] += completion_tokens
   136	        _stats["total_tokens"] += prompt_tokens + completion_tokens
   137	        _stats["requests"] += 1
   138	
   139	
   140	def _record_estimated():
   141	    with _stats_lock:
   142	        _stats["estimated_count"] += 1
   143	
   144	
   145	def _record_error():
   146	    with _stats_lock:
   147	        _stats["errors"] += 1
   148	
   149	
   150	def _record_retry():
   151	    with _stats_lock:
   152	        _stats["retries_429"] += 1
   153	
   154	
   155	def _reset_stats():
   156	    with _stats_lock:
   157	        for k in _stats:
   158	            _stats[k] = 0
   159	        for prov in _per_key_requests:
   160	            _per_key_requests[prov] = [0] * len(_per_key_requests[prov])
   161	
   162	
   163	def _get_stats():
   164	    with _stats_lock:
   165	        snap = dict(_stats)
   166	        snap["per_key_requests"] = {
   167	            p: list(v) for p, v in _per_key_requests.items()
   168	        }
   169	        return snap
   170	
   171	
   172	# ── Rate Limiter ──
   173	_rate_lock = threading.Lock()
   174	_rate_semaphore = threading.Semaphore(int(os.environ.get("LLM_PROXY_CONCURRENCY", "5")))
   175	_cooldown_until = 0.0
   176	
   177	
   178	def _wait_for_cooldown():
   179	    global _cooldown_until
   180	    now = time.time()
   181	    if now < _cooldown_until:
   182	        wait = _cooldown_until - now
   183	        log.info(f"[RATE LIMITER] Cooling down {wait:.1f}s")
   184	        time.sleep(wait)
   185	
   186	
   187	def _trigger_cooldown(seconds):
   188	    global _cooldown_until
   189	    with _rate_lock:
   190	        new_until = time.time() + seconds
   191	        if new_until > _cooldown_until:
   192	            _cooldown_until = new_until
   193	            log.warning(f"[RATE LIMITER] Global cooldown {seconds}s")
   194

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""A8e fix F3 — unit tests for src/drivers/llm_proxy.py routing logic.
     3	
     4	Codex#4 (round-1 A8 audit) caught: `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` misrouted
     5	to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check.
     6	This test file pins the routing matrix as a CI conformance gate.
     7	
     8	Also verifies the round-robin mechanic (F2) without invoking any cloud
     9	API: `_build_clients` is monkeypatched to return a list of dummy
    10	sentinels, then `get_client_round_robin` is called multiple times and
    11	the per-key counter distribution is asserted.
    12	
    13	Run: `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` (no pytest required).

    16	import sys
    17	import unittest

    33	class RoutingMatrixTests(unittest.TestCase):

    39	        self.assertEqual(llm_proxy.detect_provider("dashscope:qwen3-8b"), "dashscope")
    40	
    41	    def test_unknown_explicit_prefix_falls_through_to_heuristic(self):

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// A8e2 fix G1 — recurring conformance gate for `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py`.
     2	//
     3	// Round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) caught: `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`
     4	// existed and was in Trust Root, but was only documented as a manual
     5	// invocation. A test that does not run automatically is just
     6	// documentation — it cannot prevent the V3L-27-class regression that
     7	// Gemini's round-1 VETO targeted.
     8	//
     9	// This integration test bridges the Python proxy suite into
    10	// `cargo test --workspace` so it runs on every Rust test invocation
    11	// and on every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. The
    12	// test depends on the system `python3` interpreter being available;
    13	// if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic (A8e3 fix H6 —
    14	// Codex round-3 caught the prior soft-skip behavior that masqueraded
    15	// as a passing gate when Python was missing). Explicit opt-out:
    16	// `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` (must be set deliberately;
    17	// the bypass is logged loudly to make the gate's absence visible).
    18	//
    19	// Constitutional anchor: meta-witness for atom A7 (case C-027 +
    20	// V3L-27 mitigation — multi-key round-robin avoiding single-key
    21	// rate-limit collapse).
    22	
    23	use std::path::PathBuf;
    24	use std::process::Command;
    25	
    26	fn repo_root() -> PathBuf {
    27	    // The minif2f_v4 crate's Cargo.toml lives at the repo's
    28	    // experiments/minif2f_v4 path, so two parents up is the repo root.
    29	    PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"))
    30	        .parent()
    31	        .and_then(|p| p.parent())
    32	        .expect("repo root reachable from CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")
    33	        .to_path_buf()
    34	}
    35	
    36	#[test]
    37	fn proxy_python_conformance_suite_passes() {
    38	    let root = repo_root();
    39	    let script = root.join("scripts").join("test_llm_proxy.py");
    40	    assert!(
    41	        script.is_file(),
    42	        "scripts/test_llm_proxy.py must exist at the canonical path; got {:?}",
    43	        script
    44	    );
    45	
    46	    // A8e3 fix H6 (Codex R3#3): the wrapper MUST fail closed when
    47	    // `python3` is missing. A "soft skip" is exactly the silent-pass
    48	    // failure mode that Gemini's round-1 VETO targeted — a gate that
    49	    // disappears under environmental drift is not a gate. If a runner
    50	    // environment lacks Python, that's a CI configuration bug, not an
    51	    // acceptable-skip case. Explicit opt-out:
    52	    // `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` — must be set deliberately,
    53	    // never set by default. The bypass is logged loudly so the gate's
    54	    // absence is visible in test output.
    55	    let opt_out = std::env::var("SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE")
    56	        .as_deref() == Ok("1");
    57	    if opt_out {
    58	        eprintln!(
    59	            "[G1] SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1 — gate explicitly \
    60	             bypassed. This is a downgraded run; the V3L-27 round-robin \
    61	             conformance battery did NOT execute."
    62	        );
    63	        return;
    64	    }
    65	    let python_check = Command::new("python3").arg("--version").output();
    66	    assert!(
    67	        python_check.is_ok(),
    68	        "python3 not found on PATH; G1 conformance gate requires it. \
    69	         Install python3 + the openai SDK (see scripts/test_llm_proxy.py \
    70	         header) or — only with deliberate intent — set \
    71	         SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1 to bypass."
    72	    );
    73	
    74	    let output = Command::new("python3")
    75	        .arg(&script)
    76	        .current_dir(&root)
    77	        .output()
    78	        .expect("spawn python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py");
    79	
    80	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
    81	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
    82	
    83	    assert!(
    84	        output.status.success(),
    85	        "scripts/test_llm_proxy.py must exit 0 (round-robin + routing \
    86	         conformance battery for V3L-27 mitigation).\n\
    87	         status: {:?}\nstdout:\n{}\nstderr:\n{}",
    88	        output.status,
    89	        stdout,
    90	        stderr
    91	    );
    92	
    93	    // Sanity-check the unittest summary line is present so a future
    94	    // refactor that removes the test class definitions surfaces here
    95	    // instead of silently skipping.
    96	    assert!(
    97	        stderr.contains("OK") || stdout.contains("OK"),
    98	        "unittest output must contain the trailing 'OK' line.\n\
    99	         stdout:\n{}\nstderr:\n{}",
   100	        stdout,
   101	        stderr
   102	    );
   103	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
   195	
   196	def detect_provider(model):
   197	    """Route by model identifier. v4 prefers explicit `provider:model`
   198	    syntax (e.g. `siliconflow:Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct`); falls back
   199	    to model-string heuristics for backward compat with v3 callers.
   200	
   201	    Routing matrix (A8e fix F3 + A8e6 fix K2):
   202	      explicit `provider:...`            → that provider (if known)
   203	      slash-separated id (huggingface-style "Org/Model")
   204	                                          → siliconflow (dispatches the
   205	                                            full catalog including Qwen,
   206	                                            openai-compat, Meta, AND
   207	                                            `deepseek-ai/<distill>` SF-
   208	                                            catalog DeepSeek variants —
   209	                                            see K2 note below)
   210	      bare contains "deepseek"           → deepseek (api.deepseek.com)
   211	      bare "qwen3-*" / "qwen-*"          → dashscope (Aliyun Qwen direct)
   212	      else                                → dashscope (default fallback)
   213	
   214	    Round-1 audit caught a routing inversion: `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct`
   215	    used to misroute to dashscope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won
   216	    after the slash check. F3 promoted slash-form above bare-qwen.
   217	
   218	    Round-6 audit (Codex R6#2) caught a related collision: HuggingFace-
   219	    style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` is a SiliconFlow-
   220	    catalog model (the official DeepSeek API only serves the canonical
   221	    `deepseek-chat` / `deepseek-v4-flash` family, not the Distill
   222	    variants). The pre-K2 logic checked `"deepseek" in m` BEFORE the
   223	    slash check, so `deepseek-ai/...` misrouted to api.deepseek.com
   224	    and 404'd. K2 swaps the order: slash-form is now the FIRST routing
   225	    heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring
   226	    only fires when there's no slash.
   227	    """
   228	    if ":" in model:
   229	        prefix = model.split(":", 1)[0].lower()
   230	        if prefix in PROVIDERS:
   231	            return prefix
   232	    if "/" in model:
   233	        # Any slash-form (Qwen/..., openai/..., meta-llama/...,
   234	        # deepseek-ai/...) goes to the heterogeneous catalog provider.
   235	        # K2: this branch is FIRST so HuggingFace-style identifiers
   236	        # never silently fall through to a bare-string provider that
   237	        # doesn't actually serve them.
   238	        return "siliconflow"
   239	    m = model.lower()
   240	    if "deepseek" in m:
   241	        # Bare "deepseek-chat", "deepseek-v4-flash" → official endpoint.
   242	        # Slash-form like "deepseek-ai/..." won't reach here per K2.
   243	        return "deepseek"
   244	    if m.startswith("qwen"):
   245	        # Bare qwen3-*, qwen-* without slash = direct DashScope catalog.
   246	        return "dashscope"
   247	    return "dashscope"
   248	
   249	
   250	def strip_provider_prefix(model):
   251	    """If model is `provider:foo/bar`, return `foo/bar`; else `model`."""
   252	    if ":" in model:
   253	        prefix, rest = model.split(":", 1)
   254	        if prefix.lower() in PROVIDERS:
   255	            return rest
   256	    return model
   257	
   258	
   259	class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
   260	    def do_GET(self):
   261	        if self.path == "/health":
   262	            self._json_response(200, {"status": "ok"})
   263	        elif self.path == "/stats":
   264	            self._json_response(200, _get_stats())
   265	        else:
   266	            self.send_error(404)
   267	
   268	    def do_POST(self):
   269	        if self.path == "/stats/reset":
   270	            _reset_stats()
   271	            self._json_response(200, {"status": "reset"})
   272	            log.info("[STATS] Counters reset")
   273	            return
   274	
   275	        if "/v1/chat/completions" not in self.path:

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// PPUT-CCL Phase B B7 — Trust Root + Boot freeze (PREREG § 1.8 + § 7).
     2	//
     3	// Constitutional anchor: FC3-S3 `readonly` subgraph (constitution.md
     4	// line 670, system-level flowchart). The constitutional readonly base
     5	// is {constitution-as-ground-truth, logs-archive-as-ground-truth}; B7
     6	// extends this base per PREREG § 1.8 to also cover the case-law glob,
     7	// pre-registration spec, heldout splits, and the PPUT accounting layer.
     8	// TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC3-N34 was 📅 Phase 11+ ("FS-level readonly
     9	// check at init") — B7 implements it via SHA-256 manifest verification.
    10	// See `handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md`.
    11	//
    12	// At Boot we hash every tracked file and compare against the
    13	// `[trust_root]` manifest in `genesis_payload.toml`. Any mismatch =>
    14	// `TrustRootError::Tampered { .. }`. `src/main.rs` panics with
    15	// `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED`.
    16	//
    17	// Manifest derivation (Phase B7, independently re-derived from PREREG
    18	// § 1.8 + B2-B4 mid-term audit recommendation + B6 prompt_guard add):
    19	// see header comment in `genesis_payload.toml`.
    20	//
    21	// TOML parsing is hand-rolled (~30 LOC). The manifest format is flat:
    22	// section header + `"path" = "hash"` lines. Adding a `toml` crate
    23	// dependency would drag in ~5 transitive crates for what we can do
    24	// in-line; compression principle (CLAUDE.md "反奥利奥架构") wins.
    25	
    26	use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
    27	use std::fs;
    28	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    29	
    30	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: failure variants of the readonly-guard verification.
    31	/// Constitutional role = the diagnostic surface that distinguishes
    32	/// `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED` (real readonly violation) from `GenesisRead` /
    33	/// `GenesisParse` (manifest itself unreadable, also a violation but a
    34	/// different fix path).
    35	#[derive(Debug)]
    36	pub enum TrustRootError {
    37	    GenesisRead(std::io::Error),
    38	    GenesisParse(String),
    39	    SectionMissing(&'static str),
    40	    FileRead { path: PathBuf, err: std::io::Error },
    41	    Tampered { path: PathBuf, expected: String, actual: String },
    42	}
    43	
    44	impl std::fmt::Display for TrustRootError {
    45	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
    46	        match self {
    47	            Self::GenesisRead(e) => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: cannot read genesis_payload.toml: {e}"),
    48	            Self::GenesisParse(s) => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: genesis_payload.toml parse error: {s}"),
    49	            Self::SectionMissing(s) => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: genesis_payload.toml missing section [{s}]"),
    50	            Self::FileRead { path, err } => write!(f, "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: cannot read tracked file {}: {err}", path.display()),
    51	            Self::Tampered { path, expected, actual } => write!(
    52	                f,
    53	                "TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED: {} hash mismatch (expected {}, actual {})",
    54	                path.display(), expected, actual
    55	            ),
    56	        }
    57	    }
    58	}
    59	
    60	impl std::error::Error for TrustRootError {}
    61	
    62	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: implementation of the constitutional `readonly`
    63	/// subgraph (constitution.md FC3, system-level flowchart). Verifies every
    64	/// tracked file's SHA-256 against the `genesis_payload.toml [trust_root]`
    65	/// manifest at Boot. Mismatch => Boot abort; the readonly guarantee that
    66	/// the constitution requires of {constitution, logs} (extended per PREREG
    67	/// § 1.8 to the full PPUT-accounting base) is enforced here.
    68	///
    69	/// `repo_root` is the directory containing `genesis_payload.toml` (typically
    70	/// the workspace root). Paths in the manifest are interpreted relative to it.
    71	pub fn verify_trust_root(repo_root: &Path) -> Result<(), TrustRootError> {
    72	    let genesis_path = repo_root.join("genesis_payload.toml");
    73	    let genesis_text = fs::read_to_string(&genesis_path).map_err(TrustRootError::GenesisRead)?;
    74	    let manifest = parse_trust_root_section(&genesis_text)?;
    75	    if !has_section(&genesis_text, "pput_accounting_0") {
    76	        return Err(TrustRootError::SectionMissing("pput_accounting_0"));
    77	    }
    78	    for (rel_path, expected) in &manifest {
    79	        let full = repo_root.join(rel_path);
    80	        let bytes = fs::read(&full).map_err(|err| TrustRootError::FileRead {
    81	            path: full.clone(),
    82	            err,
    83	        })?;
    84	        let actual = hex_lower(&Sha256::digest(&bytes));
    85	        if actual != *expected {
    86	            return Err(TrustRootError::Tampered {
    87	                path: full,
    88	                expected: expected.clone(),
    89	                actual,
    90	            });
    91	        }
    92	        // Recurse into MANIFEST.sha256 children: the parent file's hash
    93	        // alone doesn't bind the children it claims (proxy was convention,
    94	        // not enforcement, before A8e13 fix Q1).
    95	        if rel_path.ends_with("/MANIFEST.sha256") {
    96	            verify_child_manifest(repo_root, &bytes)?;
    97	        }
    98	    }
    99	    Ok(())
   100	}
   101	
   102	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34 + case C-075: child-manifest recursion.
   103	/// Format = GNU `sha256sum` (`<64-hex>  <repo-relative-path>`).
   104	/// Paths resolve from `repo_root` (manifests are regenerated from
   105	/// the repo root, not from each manifest's parent dir).
   106	fn verify_child_manifest(repo_root: &Path, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<(), TrustRootError> {
   107	    let text = std::str::from_utf8(bytes)
   108	        .map_err(|e| TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("manifest not utf-8: {e}")))?;
   109	    for (i, raw) in text.lines().enumerate() {
   110	        let line = raw.trim();
   111	        if line.is_empty() || line.starts_with('#') {
   112	            continue;
   113	        }
   114	        let (hex, child_rel) = line.split_once("  ").ok_or_else(|| {
   115	            TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("manifest line {}: {line:?}", i + 1))
   116	        })?;
   117	        if hex.len() != 64 || !hex.bytes().all(|b| b.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {
   118	            return Err(TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!(
   119	                "manifest line {}: bad hex {hex:?}", i + 1
   120	            )));
   121	        }
   122	        let path = repo_root.join(child_rel);
   123	        let actual = hex_lower(&Sha256::digest(
   124	            &fs::read(&path).map_err(|err| TrustRootError::FileRead { path: path.clone(), err })?,
   125	        ));
   126	        if actual != hex.to_ascii_lowercase() {
   127	            return Err(TrustRootError::Tampered {
   128	                path,
   129	                expected: hex.to_ascii_lowercase(),
   130	                actual,
   131	            });
   132	        }
   133	    }
   134	    Ok(())
   135	}
   136	
   137	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: helper for `verify_trust_root` — exposed because
   138	/// the trust_root_immutability conformance battery (Phase B7) reads the
   139	/// manifest directly to assert it includes the audit-recommended PPUT
   140	/// accounting layer.
   141	///
   142	/// Parses the `[trust_root]` section of `genesis_payload.toml` into ordered
   143	/// `(path, sha256)` pairs. Hand-rolled — accepts the narrow subset we emit
   144	/// (quoted-key = quoted-value, comments, blank lines).
   145	pub fn parse_trust_root_section(text: &str) -> Result<Vec<(String, String)>, TrustRootError> {
   146	    let mut in_section = false;
   147	    let mut entries = Vec::new();
   148	    for (lineno, raw) in text.lines().enumerate() {
   149	        let line = strip_comment(raw).trim();
   150	        if line.is_empty() {
   151	            continue;
   152	        }
   153	        if let Some(header) = line.strip_prefix('[').and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix(']')) {
   154	            in_section = header.trim() == "trust_root";
   155	            continue;
   156	        }
   157	        if !in_section {
   158	            continue;
   159	        }
   160	        let (key, value) = line.split_once('=').ok_or_else(|| {
   161	            TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("line {}: missing '=' in [trust_root]", lineno + 1))
   162	        })?;
   163	        let key = unquote(key.trim()).ok_or_else(|| {
   164	            TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("line {}: key not quoted", lineno + 1))
   165	        })?;
   166	        let value = unquote(value.trim()).ok_or_else(|| {
   167	            TrustRootError::GenesisParse(format!("line {}: value not quoted", lineno + 1))
   168	        })?;
   169	        entries.push((key.to_string(), value.to_string()));
   170	    }
   171	    if entries.is_empty() {
   172	        return Err(TrustRootError::SectionMissing("trust_root"));
   173	    }
   174	    Ok(entries)
   175	}
   176	
   177	fn has_section(text: &str, name: &str) -> bool {
   178	    text.lines().any(|raw| {
   179	        let line = strip_comment(raw).trim();
   180	        line
   181	            .strip_prefix('[')
   182	            .and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix(']'))
   183	            .map(|h| h.trim() == name)
   184	            .unwrap_or(false)
   185	    })
   186	}
   187	
   188	fn strip_comment(line: &str) -> &str {
   189	    let mut in_string = false;
   190	    for (i, c) in line.char_indices() {
   191	        match c {
   192	            '"' => in_string = !in_string,
   193	            '#' if !in_string => return &line[..i],
   194	            _ => {}
   195	        }
   196	    }
   197	    line
   198	}
   199	
   200	fn unquote(s: &str) -> Option<&str> {
   201	    s.strip_prefix('"').and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix('"'))
   202	}
   203	
   204	fn hex_lower(bytes: &[u8]) -> String {
   205	    use std::fmt::Write;
   206	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(bytes.len() * 2);
   207	    for b in bytes {
   208	        write!(out, "{b:02x}").unwrap();
   209	    }
   210	    out

    65	    fs::write(tmp.join("genesis_payload.toml"), genesis).unwrap();
    66	    fs::write(tmp.join("only.txt"), "tampered content").unwrap();

     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# Codex Phase A → B exit audit — covers A0–A7 deliverables.
     3	# Independent of Gemini.
     4	set -euo pipefail
     5	
     6	ROOT="/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4"
     7	# A8_AUDIT_ROUND is REQUIRED (A8e10 fix O1, Codex R9#1). Earlier the
     8	# script defaulted to R2, which silently overwrote the round-2
     9	# transcript on unattended re-runs. Now: explicit env var or fail
    10	# fast. Round-1 (no suffix) lives at the un-suffixed transcript;
    11	# round 2+ uses _R2/_R3/... per chronology.
    12	if [ -z "${A8_AUDIT_ROUND:-}" ]; then
    13	    echo "[run_codex_a8_exit] error: A8_AUDIT_ROUND env var is required" >&2
    14	    echo "    usage: A8_AUDIT_ROUND=R<n> bash $0" >&2
    15	    echo "    sets the suffix for handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_<round>.md" >&2
    16	    exit 2
    17	fi
    18	ROUND="$A8_AUDIT_ROUND"
    19	OUT="${ROOT}/handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_${ROUND}.md"
    20	if [ -f "$OUT" ]; then
    21	    echo "[run_codex_a8_exit] error: $OUT already exists; refusing to overwrite" >&2
    22	    echo "    (prior audit transcripts are append-only governance artifacts;" >&2
    23	    echo "    delete the file explicitly if you really intend to re-run round $ROUND)" >&2
    24	    exit 2
    25	fi
    26	TMP_PROMPT="$(mktemp /tmp/codex_a8_exit.XXXXXX.md)"
    27	trap 'rm -f "$TMP_PROMPT"' EXIT
    28	
    29	PACKET="${ROOT}/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md"
    30	HISTORY="${ROOT}/handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md"
    31	
    32	cat > "$TMP_PROMPT" <<'BRIEF_EOF'
    33	# Codex Phase A → B Exit Audit (PPUT-CCL arc)
    34	
    35	**Role**: skeptical adversarial reviewer. Independent of Gemini. Conservative merge rule (memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS.
    36	
    37	**Mandate**: Phase A is pre-flight (days 1–3 of the 30-day arc). 8 atoms (A0a–e + A1–A7) must be auditable as a unit before Phase B (kernel instrumentation + PPUT accounting) is authorized to start. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md (round-4 PASS/PASS, frozen) + PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md (Trust Root entry 25) are the contracts you're auditing against.
    38	
    39	The packet below is self-contained. Read it as a standalone document — your conclusions go to ArchitectAI, who will iterate on CHALLENGE items in the same audit cycle. The Phase A0 exit audit (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE → 7 fixes) is the precedent for how rigorous to be.
    40	
    41	End your audit with:
    42	- **VERDICT**: PASS / CHALLENGE / VETO
    43	- **Conviction**: low / medium / high
    44	- **Recommendation**: PROCEED to Phase B / FIX-THEN-PROCEED / REDESIGN
    45	
    46	Cite §/file:line for every finding. Be specific about which atom and which line.
    47	
    48	---
    49	
    50	BRIEF_EOF
    51	
    52	# Append the packet itself + the audit history (append-only chronology
    53	# companion document — reviewers needing round-N closure context find it
    54	# there; the packet is current-state only post-A8e7 structural rewrite).
    55	cat "$PACKET" >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    56	printf '\n\n---\n\n# Audit history (append-only chronology)\n\n' >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    57	cat "$HISTORY" >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    58	
    59	# Append source files referenced by the packet so the auditor can verify
    60	# without round-tripping to the repo. Order: most-load-bearing first.
    61	printf '\n\n---\n\n# Appended source files\n\n' >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    62	
    63	for f in \
    64	    handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md \
    65	    experiments/minif2f_v4/src/agent_models.rs \
    66	    experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs \
    67	    experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs \
    68	    experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs \
    69	    experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs \
    70	    experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs \
    71	    src/drivers/llm_proxy.py \
    72	    scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh \
    73	    scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py \
    74	    scripts/test_llm_proxy.py \
    75	    experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs \
    76	    experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/trust_root_immutability.rs \
    77	    experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs \
    78	    experiments/minif2f_v4/examples/fc_trace_emit_one.rs \
    79	    handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md \
    80	    genesis_payload.toml ; do
    81	    case "$f" in
    82	        *.rs) lang="rust" ;;
    83	        *.py) lang="python" ;;
    84	        *.sh) lang="bash" ;;
    85	        *.toml) lang="toml" ;;
    86	        *.yaml|*.yml) lang="yaml" ;;
    87	        *) lang="" ;;
    88	    esac
    89	    printf '\n## %s\n\n```%s\n' "$f" "$lang" >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    90	    cat "${ROOT}/${f}" >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    91	    printf '\n```\n' >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    92	done
    93	
    94	printf '\n---\n\nGive your INDEPENDENT Phase A → B exit audit. Cite §/file:line for every finding.\n' >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
    95	
    96	prompt_size=$(wc -c < "$TMP_PROMPT")
    97	echo "[codex a8 exit] prompt size: ${prompt_size} chars" >&2
    98	
    99	t0=$(date +%s)
   100	{
   101	  printf '# Codex Phase A → B Exit Audit (PPUT-CCL arc)\n'
   102	  printf '**Round**: %s\n' "$ROUND"
   103	  printf '**Date**: 2026-04-26\n'
   104	  printf '**Reference**: see `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` for full chronology including atom commit chain + per-round verdicts/fixes.\n'
   105	  printf '**Test baseline**: 267 PASS + 29 ignored + 0 failed (Rust); 16/16 PASS (Python proxy tests)\n'
   106	  printf '**Trust Root**: 38-entry manifest verifies clean\n'
   107	  printf '**Prompt size**: %s chars\n\n---\n\n' "$prompt_size"
   108	  codex exec --skip-git-repo-check < "$TMP_PROMPT" 2>&1
   109	} > "$OUT"
   110	t1=$(date +%s)
   111	elapsed=$((t1 - t0))
   112	echo "[codex a8 exit] done in ${elapsed}s, saved: $OUT" >&2

    39	def load_env() -> dict[str, str]:

--- 6be6eb4 A1: PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24->25
A1: PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24->25

FC-trace: PREREG (5.5 deferral; protocol unchanged) + Art. V.1.2
          (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution PREREG) +
          C-073 + C-075 (tool qualification scope)

Phase A engineering atom 1 of 8 per the 2026-04-25 auto-research plan.

WHY DEFER

PREREG (5.5 estimated 576-run calibration as 8 hours, $3-5. Empirical
observation 2026-04-25 (during launched batch 650caf7 era):
- Per-run wall-clock ~15-30 min (was estimated ~50s) — 30-40x off
- Total batch 3-7 days realistic (was estimated 8h) — 9-21x off
- Cost still cheap (~$15-20)

The 8h estimate was based on chat-oneshot speed assumption that
proved wrong for swarm n3 on adaptation-144 problem mix (many aime
problems hit max_transactions=200 ceiling).

User mid-session 2026-04-25 explicitly questioned 576-run necessity
given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents to PPUT
relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth
feedback pipeline).
--- 180a300 A2: P0a swarm_N=1 mode + parse_swarm_condition_n unit tests
A2: P0a swarm_N=1 mode + parse_swarm_condition_n unit tests

FC-trace: FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration (oneshot vs swarm discriminator)
          + FC1-N11 (swarm path traverses ∏p product; oneshot does not)

Phase A engineering atom 2 of 8 per the 2026-04-25 auto-research plan.

KEY FINDING — already exists, just needs witness

CONDITION=n1 already routes to run_swarm with n_agents=1 in the
existing generic n* parser (evaluator.rs:226 pre-fix). Effectively
swarm_N=1 mode has been available since the original n* code path
landed. A2 deliverable is the WITNESS that this routing is testable
+ documented as the correct N=1 baseline.

CHANGES

1. Refactored inline parser to pub(crate) parse_swarm_condition_n
   helper function with explicit doc-comment FC-trace.
   - Returns Some(N) for "n<digits>" with N >= 1
   - Returns None for oneshot, hybrid_v1, malformed inputs
   - Filters n0 (zero agents = degenerate) to None

2. Added 5 unit tests in inline cfg(test) mod swarm_condition_tests:
--- 7f4bc0c A3: per-agent AGENT_MODELS env var (Phase B+C single-model gate)
A3: per-agent AGENT_MODELS env var (Phase B+C single-model gate)

FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI canonical identity) + Phase B+C single-model
          invariant (notepad F-2026-04-25-02 + memory feedback_phased_checkpoint)

Phase A atom A3 — capability for binding distinct δ per Agent_i in the
swarm path. Default behavior (env unset) broadcasts the global model
from ACTIVE_MODEL to every agent_idx, preserving Phase B current
behavior bit-for-bit. Heterogeneous payloads (≥2 distinct models in
the resolved vector) are gated by PHASE_D_HETERO_OK=1; without the
gate the resolver aborts at startup before any LLM call.

NEW MODULE
- experiments/minif2f_v4/src/agent_models.rs:
  - parse_agent_models(env_str) — pure CSV parser, empty/blank → []
  - expand_agent_models(parsed, global, n_agents, hetero_gated) — pure
    validator + expander; broadcast/positional + Phase B/C gate
  - resolve_agent_models(global, n_agents) — env-coupled wrapper
  - AgentModelsError {LengthMismatch, EmptyEntry, HeterogeneousWithoutGate}
  - 10 unit tests (broadcast / positional / length mismatch / empty
    entry / hetero-gated / hetero-ungated / uniform-length-N)

WIRING (run_swarm only — run_oneshot is single-call)
- evaluator.rs: resolve once at run_swarm entry, fail-fast on error.
--- a5c78e4 A4: decomposed metrics (hit_max_tx + tactic_diversity + verifier_wait_ms)
A4: decomposed metrics (hit_max_tx + tactic_diversity + verifier_wait_ms)

Phase A engineering atom 4 of 8 — per Codex/Gemini N-agents brainstorms 2026-04-25:
PPUT alone conflates solve_rate × tokens_per_solve × time_per_solve. Decompose at the
emit boundary so downstream analysis can split the three components and surface
mechanism-level effects (Amdahl/USL serial-vs-parallel, search-party diversity,
budget-exhaustion vs accept).

Three new non-Optional v2 fields on RunAggregate + PputResult:

- hit_max_tx (bool): true iff the run reached max_transactions WITHOUT OMEGA AND
  without firing the B7-extra synthetic short-circuit. Disambiguates natural budget
  exhaustion (FC2-N22 HALT via MaxTxExhausted) from OMEGA accept at the same
  tx_count and from the calibration-treatment short-circuit (which exits ~150 tx
  EARLY at the rollback threshold; tagged via synthetic_short_circuit instead).

- tactic_diversity (f64 ∈ [0, 1]): distinct-payload-hash / total over every parsed
  append/complete/step proposal in the run. Cheap proxy for the semantic-diversity
  metric the brainstorms proposed; embedding distance is Phase D+ work. Generalizes
  the existing C-036 unique_payload_ratio (which only counts OMEGA attempts).

- verifier_wait_ms (u64): cumulative wall-clock inside Lean (verify_omega /
  verify_omega_detailed / verify_partial). Strict sub-interval of total_wall_time_ms
  by construction. Enables the Amdahl/USL decomposition Codex § C proposed.
--- 30f2a14 A5: per-agent budget normalization (BUDGET_REGIME + MAX_TRANSACTIONS env vars)
A5: per-agent budget normalization (BUDGET_REGIME + MAX_TRANSACTIONS env vars)

FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (delta
instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime).

PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer 2026-04-25 sec 3 condition 3 satisfied: every
emitted v2 row now stamps the budget partitioning rule + base, so
MaxTxExhausted rows are unambiguous across N values when calibration
eventually runs. Default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline
(total_proposal x 200) bit-for-bit.

New module experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs:
- BUDGET_REGIME env var (total_proposal | per_agent | token_total |
  wall_clock); default total_proposal.
- MAX_TRANSACTIONS env var; default 200.
- 4-variant enum + pure parse_budget_regime / parse_max_transactions /
  effective_max_tx + env-coupled resolve_budget.
- Phase A scope = total_proposal + per_agent implemented; latter two
  declared startup-fatal UnimplementedRegime so a misconfigured run
  aborts before any LLM call.
- Per-agent regime: effective_max_tx = base x n_agents
  (Codex/Gemini brainstorm A.3 "N x tx = constant" reframed as
  constant per-agent).

--- 89994c7 A6: per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events (fc_trace module)
A6: per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events (fc_trace module)

FC-trace: meta-witness for FC1 / FC2 / FC3 path coverage. Closes
TRACE_MATRIX_v2 sec 5 item 7 (was the last open Phase A scope item
before A7/A8).

Codex N-agents brainstorm 2026-04-25 sec D recommended OpenTelemetry-
style structured JSONL events tagged with FC IDs so Phase D ArchitectAI
can replay the constitutional path without source access. Implementation
chose pure stdlib over the tracing crate to avoid a new dep tree; the
emit_event + FcId surface is small enough that Phase D+ can swap to a
tracing-subscriber bridge locally.

New module experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs:
- 7-variant FcId enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20
  / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). as_str produces dash-form labels Phase D
  consumers + TRACE_MATRIX rows join on.
- FC_TRACE=1 gate, cached in OnceLock (single env::var read at first
  call; zero per-event overhead in production where the gate is off).
- FC_TRACE_FILE=path redirects emit to file (default sink stderr).
- Hand-rolled minimal JSON encoder (json_str escapes RFC 8259 sec 7
  chars + control chars to \uXXXX).
- emit_event(fc, run_id, tx, agent_id, kv) writes one line.

--- 90953d6 A7: SiliconFlow heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing (proxy + 3-key smoke)
A7: SiliconFlow heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing (proxy + 3-key smoke)

FC-trace: FC1-N7 (delta/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous delta
instances across SiliconFlow catalog enable Phase D meta-loop).

User correction 2026-04-26: SiliconFlow is the project's heterogeneous-
LLM lane, NOT a fallback / probe-only target. Three keys provided for
high-concurrency multi-key round-robin. Memory reference_siliconflow.md
records this lesson (context-loss anti-pattern: check .env + project
files BEFORE asking the user where credentials live).

Plumbing brought into v4 from v3:
- src/drivers/llm_proxy.py — OpenAI-compatible local HTTP server with
  one load-bearing v4 change vs v3 fork: per-provider multi-key
  round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env-var names per
  provider; clients_by_provider builds one OpenAI client per available
  key; get_client_round_robin distributes via _rr_counters mod len.
  Three SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate
  rate-limit pools (mitigates V3L-27 / case C-027 single-key N=30
  401/429 collapse documented in cases/V3_LESSONS.md).
- detect_provider() now recognizes explicit `provider:model` syntax
  (e.g. siliconflow:Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) for unambiguous routing
  in AGENT_MODELS payloads.
- /stats endpoint exposes per_key_requests for observability.

handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:379:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.

FC-trace: FC1-N7 (delta/AI prompt construction — R2 fixes the
search_cache injected into every prompt; that's the prompt that
delta receives) + meta governance (R1 numbers cascade).

Round-12 verdict (per memory feedback_dual_audit_conflict):
- handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md: CHALLENGE / high

- Merged: CHALLENGE. Codex caught 3 stale-number cascade items;
  Gemini surfaced 1 new substantive run_swarm bug via independent
  code review.

Two fixes:

R1 (Codex R12#1+#2+#3 — stale 37/265 cascade): A8e13 bumped TR
manifest 37 to 38 (added src/boot.rs + 2 new boot tests) but
didn't propagate the new numbers to:
- A8 packet § 2 metric table (`265` → `267`, `37` → `38`)
- A8 packet § 4 Phase B checklist (37-entry → 38-entry; added
  recursive-enforcement note per Q1)
- A8 packet § 6 Q4.a (265 → 267)
- handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh:105-106
  printf header (Trust-Rooted gate machinery)
- handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py:153-155
  same printf header
- TRACE_MATRIX § 3 "rules/MANIFEST.sha256 (proxy for 14 rules)"
  → "15 rules + Q1 ENFORCED proxy" note
- TRACE_MATRIX § 6 chain extended with A8e13 → 38
- TRACE_MATRIX milestones bullet list extended
- genesis_payload.toml header progression chain extended with
  A8e13=38 + recursive-enforcement note

R2 (Gemini R12 — substantive run_swarm bug): when an agent's
search_count hits SEARCH_CAP (default 20), the search tool is
correctly stripped from tools_desc. BUT search_cache[agent] kept
being injected into every subsequent prompt's hits_ref for the
rest of the run. Agent reasons from stale data forever; would
silently confound any swarm experiment that exercises the cap.

Fix at experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:870-885:
single `cap_hit` boolean gates BOTH the tool list AND the cache
injection. When cap_hit:
  hits_ref = Vec::new()
  tools_desc = "append, complete, invest"
Otherwise:
  hits_ref = search_cache[agent_id].cloned()
  tools_desc = "append, complete, invest, search"

Single source of truth means the two cannot drift apart.

Trust Root manifest unchanged at 38 (only re-hashes). Re-hashed:
- experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs (R2 search_cache fix)
- handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md (Round 12 + A8e14
  + A8e13b sealed)
- handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh (R1 header bump)
- handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py (R1 header bump)

## Verifications (per C-076 Rule 1 / R-020 discipline)

Verified R1 packet metric table: grep "267" + "38" in
  A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md § 2 returns the bumped values;
  no remaining "265 PASS" or "37-entry" hits in current-state
  files (only in frozen historical transcripts and TRACE_MATRIX
  A8eN retrospective entries which correctly state what was true
  at the time).

Verified R1 runner header: grep "267 PASS" + "38-entry" in
  run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh and run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py
  returns 4 hits (2 per file).

Verified R1 TRACE_MATRIX milestone chain: tail of § 6 milestones
  shows "A8e13 src/boot.rs ... → **38**" + "(planned) B7-extra
  calibration freeze → 39"; no "→ **37**" final marker.

Verified R1 genesis header: head of [trust_root] section comment
  ends with "A8e13=38 (src/boot.rs ...) ... per A8e13 Q1 the
  {cases,rules}/MANIFEST.sha256 entries are now ENFORCED proxies".

   292	
   293	    #[test]
   294	    fn verify_trust_root_passes_when_hash_matches_in_tempdir() {
   295	        let tmp = tempdir();
   296	        let payload = "hello";
   297	        let hash = hex_lower(&Sha256::digest(payload.as_bytes()));
   298	        write_single_entry_repo(&tmp, payload, &hash);
   299	        verify_trust_root(&tmp).expect("matching hash verifies");
   300	    }
   301	
   302	    fn tempdir() -> PathBuf {
   303	        // Minimal tempdir without adding a `tempfile` dep.
   304	        let pid = std::process::id();
   305	        let nano = std::time::SystemTime::now()
   306	            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   307	            .unwrap()
   308	            .as_nanos();
   309	        let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("turingosv4-boot-test-{pid}-{nano}"));
   310	        fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
   311	        dir
   312	    }
   313	
   314	    /// A8e13 fix Q1 conformance: child manifest tamper is detected even
   315	    /// when the parent manifest hash itself is unchanged. This is the
   316	    /// scenario Codex R11#1 surfaced — pre-Q1, an attacker (or careless
   317	    /// developer) could edit a `cases/*.yaml` without regenerating
   318	    /// `cases/MANIFEST.sha256`, and boot would still pass because boot
   319	    /// only checked the manifest file's own hash, not its child entries.
   320	    #[test]
   321	    fn verify_trust_root_detects_child_manifest_tamper() {
   322	        let tmp = tempdir();
   323	        // Lay out a fake repo with a child manifest pointing at one
   324	        // child file. The PARENT manifest is hashed correctly into the
   325	        // [trust_root] section, but the CHILD file's actual content is
   326	        // tampered relative to what the parent manifest claims.
   327	        // Manifest paths are repo-relative per the project convention.
   328	        fs::create_dir_all(tmp.join("subdir")).unwrap();
   329	        fs::write(tmp.join("subdir/child.txt"), "tampered_content").unwrap();
   330	        let parent_text = format!("{}  subdir/child.txt\n", "0".repeat(64));
   331	        fs::write(tmp.join("subdir/MANIFEST.sha256"), &parent_text).unwrap();
   332	        let parent_hash = hex_lower(&Sha256::digest(parent_text.as_bytes()));
   333	        let genesis = format!(
   334	            "[pput_accounting_0]\nschema_version = \"1.0\"\n\n\
   335	             [trust_root]\n\"subdir/MANIFEST.sha256\" = \"{parent_hash}\"\n"
   336	        );
   337	        fs::write(tmp.join("genesis_payload.toml"), genesis).unwrap();
   338	        match verify_trust_root(&tmp).expect_err("child tamper must be detected") {
   339	            TrustRootError::Tampered { path, expected, actual } => {
   340	                assert!(path.ends_with("subdir/child.txt"),
   341	                    "expected error on child.txt, got: {path:?}");
   342	                assert_eq!(expected, "0".repeat(64));
   343	                assert_ne!(actual, expected);
   344	            }
   345	            other => panic!("expected Tampered on child, got {other:?}"),
   346	        }
   347	    }
   348	
   349	    /// Q1 conformance: child manifest with matching hashes verifies cleanly.
   350	    #[test]
   351	    fn verify_trust_root_passes_with_matching_child_manifest() {
   352	        let tmp = tempdir();
   353	        fs::create_dir_all(tmp.join("subdir")).unwrap();
   354	        let child_content = "the actual child content";
   355	        fs::write(tmp.join("subdir/child.txt"), child_content).unwrap();
   356	        let child_hash = hex_lower(&Sha256::digest(child_content.as_bytes()));
   357	        let parent_text = format!("{child_hash}  subdir/child.txt\n");
   358	        fs::write(tmp.join("subdir/MANIFEST.sha256"), &parent_text).unwrap();
   359	        let parent_hash = hex_lower(&Sha256::digest(parent_text.as_bytes()));
   360	        let genesis = format!(
   361	            "[pput_accounting_0]\nschema_version = \"1.0\"\n\n\
   362	             [trust_root]\n\"subdir/MANIFEST.sha256\" = \"{parent_hash}\"\n"
   363	        );
   364	        fs::write(tmp.join("genesis_payload.toml"), genesis).unwrap();
   365	        verify_trust_root(&tmp).expect("matching parent + child verifies");
   366	    }
   367	}
