handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5401:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5405:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5411:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5717:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5723:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5739:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:4:**FC-trace**: FC1-N12 (∏p ground-truth oracle scope unchanged) + Art. V.1.2 (commit authority) + C-073 + C-075.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5740:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:19:**The 8-hour estimate was based on "~50s/run chat oneshot" assumption that turned out wrong for swarm n3 condition on the adaptation-144 problem mix.** A 7-day batch is not "overnight"; user (mid-session 2026-04-25) explicitly questioned 576-run necessity given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents → PPUT relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth feedback pipeline, etc.).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5741:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5742:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5743:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:42:1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5744:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:46:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5745:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:61:- ✅ Phase A0 harness modernization complete (post-2026-04-25 governance work)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5746:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:77:`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` itself is **NOT EDITED** by this amendment. It remains the immutable round-4 frozen pre-registration. This amendment is a separate document referenced from § 5.5 forward via a pointer added to Trust Root manifest.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5821:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "strictest|least-strict|LEAST|Phase E|calibration runs|PRE-REQUISITES|operationally permitted|Type-I|accepted because|ensure" handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md' in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5823:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:62:- **Statistical framing**: `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows. `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. The substitution is operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; if/when § 3 re-calibration conditions complete, calibration upgrades the bar.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5824:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:143:- (Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording: is `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 + § 8 internally consistent + statistically correct (least-strict admissible; no Type-I inflation; substitution operative at any phase including Phase E)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5826:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:23:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5827:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5828:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:30:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5829:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5830:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5831:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:34:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5832:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:50:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5833:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:65:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5834:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:94:- Does the amendment violate any PREREG § 5.5 constraint? (Should not — defer is operationally permitted given § 5.5 ceiling.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5835:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5836:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:96:- Does deferral leave any phase blocked indefinitely? (Should not — § 3 lists explicit re-calibration conditions; if those never met, Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution per § 2 final paragraph.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:107:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:248:5. (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` calls `p_0=0.10` substitution "strictest possible substitute" — backwards (0.10 is least-strict admissible for `j-RR ≤ p_0`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:259:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 reworded: `p_0=0.10` → least-strict admissible ceiling; explicit Type-I implications paragraph added. Closes #5.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:423:## handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:426:# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:436:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:448:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:450:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:452:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:455:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:456:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:457:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the conditions in § 3 ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E, so the loose substitution never reaches the artifact-acceptance moment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:459:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:467:1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:475:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:481:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:484:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:487:- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:490:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:496:- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:510:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:520:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5269:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5272:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5278:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5323:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6269:     1	# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6279:    11	PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6291:    23	PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6293:    25	**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6295:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6298:    30	- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6299:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6300:    32	- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the conditions in § 3 ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E, so the loose substitution never reaches the artifact-acceptance moment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6302:    34	**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6310:    42	1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6318:    50	5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6324:    56	PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6327:    59	- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6330:    62	- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6333:    65	Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6339:    71	- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6353:    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6363:    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6385:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6386:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:92:Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6387:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:22:-   **(Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording**: **PASS**. The current text in `handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 and § 8 is internally consistent and statistically correct. It accurately describes `p_0 = 0.10` as the "LEAST strict admissible ceiling" and correctly states that the substitution is "operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E". The history document shows this was a point of significant contention, requiring fixes in rounds 1, 2, 7, and 8 to achieve this level of clarity and correctness. The final state is sound.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:3:# GATE: PASS/CHALLENGE/VETO BEFORE running the 576-run p_0 calibration batch
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:4:# (~$3-5 API + ~8h wall-clock + freezes p_0 into Trust Root — irreversible cost).
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:19:**Mandate**: PRE-BATCH gate. The 576-run p_0 calibration is about to launch (~$3-5 API spend, ~8 wall-hours, output freezes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` AND becomes part of Trust Root). Any defect found BEFORE the batch is cheap; any defect found AFTER means recomputing the entire calibration (= retread the full $3-5 + 8h + Trust Root churn). PASS only if you would stake your independence on the batch result being valid.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:40:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:71:### Q3 — p_0 calibration semantics
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:73:PREREG § 5.5 specifies `p_0 = sum_p max_seed(regression_p) / 144` where `regression_p = 1 iff control SOLVED && treatment UNSOLVED`. Treatment short-circuits at tx 50.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:76:- (Q3.a) The synthetic short-circuit at tx 50 measures "how often does control take ≥ 50 tx to solve". Is that a FAIR proxy for "how often does a Phase E artifact corrupt mid-run state"? An artifact-induced failure at tx 50 in a real Phase E run would produce a different cost/time profile than our short-circuit. Does this matter for j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail?
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:77:- (Q3.b) `compute_p0.py` per-problem uses `max(over seeds)` — worst-case framing. If only 1 of 2 seeds regressed, the problem still counts as 1. With 2 seeds, this DOUBLES the regression rate vs `mean(over seeds)`. PREREG § 5.5 line 450 explicitly says max — agreed?
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:78:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:79:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:80:- (Q3.e) Boltzmann seeds [31415, 2718] frozen in PREREG; `run_p0_calibration.sh` hardcodes them. If a future drift forces re-calibration, are the seeds in Trust Root manifest sufficient to detect tampering, or could a runner script tamper go undetected?
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:97:- (Q5.a) Do these gaps affect p_0 calibration RESULT (not Phase D consumption)? `compute_p0.py` joins on calibration_problem_id + calibration_seed and reads only `solved` / `progress_verified` — neither field comes from WAL or recent_rejections. Argument for "no impact": the calibration jsonl IS ground-truth-validated independently. Verify.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:99:- (Q5.c) `failed_branch_count` and `total_run_token_count` for treatment-short-circuit rows are stamped at tx 50 (correctly reflects 50 tx of real activity). Does anything downstream INTERPRET these as "if they were lower than control, the treatment toggle is bug-prone"? compute_p0.py doesn't; flag if any other tooling does.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:103:PREREG § 5.5 ceiling: if measured p_0 > 0.10, ABORT. compute_p0.py exits 2 if ceiling violated.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:106:- (Q6.a) Is exit-2 actually an ABORT in the runner workflow, or could a downstream script swallow it and continue? Test by inspection: where in the pipeline does compute_p0.py run, and what handles exit code?
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:107:- (Q6.b) p_0 > 0.10 means treatment failed too aggressively. The mitigation per PREREG = "redesign rollback simulation, redo". Are we confident the synthetic-short-circuit-at-tx-50 design is FAR enough from the ceiling that we won't hit it? If most adaptation problems take >50 tx to solve, p_0 will be HIGH. Pre-batch evidence (smoke) doesn't tell us this.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:108:- (Q6.c) If p_0 is in (0.05, 0.10] — borderline — is that a passive accept, or should a borderline result trigger automatic re-calibration with a different threshold (e.g., tx 75) to verify p_0 is stable?
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:115:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?

handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:36:-   **(RQ2.a) Synthetic Row Correctness:** The `emit_synthetic_unsolved` function in `run_p0_calibration.sh` correctly generates a JSONL row with `"solved": false`, `"progress": 0`, and `"synthetic_timeout_or_crash": true`. The `solved()` function in `compute_p0.py` correctly interprets this as UNSOLVED by checking `int(row["progress"]) == 1`. The smoke test evidence confirms the treatment run produced a row with `solved: false` and `synthetic_short_circuit: true`, which is handled by the same logic.
handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:38:    -   An evaluator crash (`exit != 0` and `exit != 124`) now aborts the entire batch (`run_p0_calibration.sh`, line 406). This correctly prevents crashes from being silently absorbed as UNSOLVED data, a P0 defect caught by Codex in Round 2.
handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:39:    -   A malformed run (`exit == 0` but no `PPUT_RESULT` line) is now explicitly caught and aborts the batch with `exit 5` (`run_p0_calibration.sh`, line 385). This resolves my Round-3 CHALLENGE.
handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:`compute_p0.py` was updated to fail loudly on incomplete or malformed data sets.
handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:49:-   **(RQ3.a) Exit Code Handling:** `compute_p0.py` exits with status 1 on data integrity failures (e.g., missing problems, duplicate rows). The runner script (`run_p0_calibration.sh`, lines 463-465) correctly distinguishes this from a successful run (exit 0) and a ceiling violation (exit 2), propagating the error code and printing a specific diagnostic.
handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:50:-   **(RQ3.b) Parameter Bypass:** The `compute()` function in `compute_p0.py` uses hardcoded `PREREG_` constants as default arguments. The `main()` function calls `compute()` without overriding these defaults, and the script's `argparse` does not expose them as command-line flags. This prevents a caller from accidentally or maliciously bypassing the pre-registered constraints.
handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:59:-   **(RQ4.b) `compute_p0.py` Honoring Equivalence:** The claim that `compute_p0.py` honors this narrow equivalence is verified. Its `solved()` function depends only on the `progress` field, which directly maps to the `solved` status. It does not inspect cost, `tx_count`, or any other non-equivalent field.

PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md (round-4 PASS/PASS, frozen) + PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md (Trust Root entry 25) are the contracts you're auditing against.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:62:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:95:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:120:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. Acceptable uses: round-1/2/3 retrospective text describing what the packet USED to say.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:183:- File: `handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:232:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → **DEFERRED with substitution per amendment § 2**: `p_0 = 0.10` hardcoded at every Gate H consumer.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:235:- ✅ Tools qualified per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification): `runner.sh`, `compute_p0.py`, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:255:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:305:## handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:308:# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:318:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:330:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:332:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:337:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:338:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:339:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the conditions in § 3 ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E, so the loose substitution never reaches the artifact-acceptance moment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:341:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:349:1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:357:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:363:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:369:- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:372:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:378:- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5104:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 25 → 26.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5154:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5415:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:4:**FC-trace**: FC1-N12 (∏p ground-truth oracle scope unchanged) + Art. V.1.2 (commit authority) + C-073 + C-075.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5416:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5417:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:92:Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5420:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:22:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.

No further verification needed.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5884:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:6381:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7657:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 25 → 26.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7661:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

Conservative merge → CHALLENGE.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:101:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:102:- **Statistical framing**: `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows. `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. The substitution is operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; if/when § 3 re-calibration conditions complete, calibration upgrades the bar.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:159:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → **DEFERRED with substitution per amendment § 2**: `p_0 = 0.10` hardcoded at every Gate H consumer.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:162:- ✅ Tools qualified per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification): `runner.sh`, `compute_p0.py`, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:183:- (Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording: is `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 + § 8 internally consistent + statistically correct (least-strict admissible; no Type-I inflation; substitution operative at any phase including Phase E)?

V.3 amendment log; mirrors `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` (frozen) + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` (delta). Per C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the gate machinery itself must be qualifiable; this history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:245:5. (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` calls `p_0=0.10` substitution "strictest possible substitute" — backwards (0.10 is least-strict admissible for `j-RR ≤ p_0`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:256:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 reworded: `p_0=0.10` → least-strict admissible ceiling; explicit Type-I implications paragraph added. Closes #5.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:439:1. (**Codex R8#1 — substantive**) PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8's audit-requirements text still contains the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Logical contradiction with the post-M4 § 2 + § 3 framing.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:568:## handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:571:# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:581:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:593:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:595:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:597:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:600:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:601:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:602:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:604:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:612:1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:620:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:626:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:629:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:632:- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:635:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:641:- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:655:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:665:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:5562:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:5568:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:5570:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:5576:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:5621:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |
