handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6024:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:23:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6025:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6026:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6027:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6028:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:34:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6029:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:50:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6030:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:65:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6031:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:94:- Does the amendment violate any PREREG § 5.5 constraint? (Should not — defer is operationally permitted given § 5.5 ceiling.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6032:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:6033:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:96:- Does deferral leave any phase blocked indefinitely? (Should not — § 3 lists explicit re-calibration conditions; if those never met, Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution per § 2 final paragraph. Calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete; absence of calibration is not a Phase E blocker.)

No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:340:## handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:343:# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:353:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:365:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:367:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:369:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:372:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:373:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:374:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the conditions in § 3 ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E, so the loose substitution never reaches the artifact-acceptance moment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:376:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:384:1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:392:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:398:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5010:        "handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md",

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5139:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5146:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5191:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5481:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_REAUDIT_2026-04-25.md:71:    -   Since `compute_p0.py` is now in the Trust Root manifest, its hash is fixed. Any execution of the calibration batch is guaranteed to run this specific version of the script, where the PREREG constants are effectively hard-coded into the `main` entry point. This prevents bypass.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5507:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:81:- (Q3.e) Boltzmann seeds [31415, 2718] frozen in PREREG; `run_p0_calibration.sh` hardcodes them. If a future drift forces re-calibration, are the seeds in Trust Root manifest sufficient to detect tampering, or could a runner script tamper go undetected?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5511:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:108:- (Q6.b) p_0 > 0.10 means treatment failed too aggressively. If most adaptation problems take >50 tx to solve, p_0 will be HIGH. Pre-batch evidence (smoke) doesn't tell us this.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5512:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:109:- (Q6.c) If p_0 is in (0.05, 0.10] — borderline — is that a passive accept, or should a borderline result trigger automatic re-calibration?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5537:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:51:- Trust Root manifest 16 → 20 entries (audit additions: src/main.rs, Cargo.lock, run_p0_calibration.sh, compute_p0.py)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5542:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:80:Runner now emits a synthetic UNSOLVED row with `synthetic_timeout_or_crash: true` instead of dropping to MEASUREMENT_ERROR. compute_p0.py strict-completeness ensures every (problem, seed) pair is present in both modes.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5544:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:90:- (RQ3.a) When ANY strict-completeness check fails, compute_p0.py exits 1 (sys.exit with string). The runner propagates exit codes: 0 → freeze; 2 → ABORT; * → "investigate". Is exit 1 (data integrity failure) handled distinctly from exit 2 (ceiling violation)?

PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md (round-4 PASS/PASS, frozen) + PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md (Trust Root entry 25) are the contracts you're auditing against.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5615:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:62:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5623:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:95:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5632:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:120:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5634:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:132:- **A1**: PREREG amendment p_0 calibration deferral + Trust Root 24 → 25.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5639:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:149:6be6eb4  A1:  PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24 → 25
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5647:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5662:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:255:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5673:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5679:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5750:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5104:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 25 → 26.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5828:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5569:   109	6be6eb4  A1:  PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24 → 25
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5838:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5604:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5857:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5675:   215	- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5882:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5751:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

(Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5987:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6747:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "conservative|strictest|strict" handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md genesis_payload.toml handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md' in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5991:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6752:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5993:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6754:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6122:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8139:/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"run_corr_id|oneshot_\\{problem_file\\}|strictest possible substitute|strictest plausible bar|conservative upper|Mathematically conservative|FC1-N12 was only|only in run_oneshot|only in oneshot|soft skip|skips with a clear diagnostic\" experiments/minif2f_v4/src handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6123:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8142:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6127:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8146:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6130:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8149:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:22:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6166:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:63:- Trust Root manifest 16 → 20 entries (audit additions: src/main.rs, Cargo.lock, run_p0_calibration.sh, compute_p0.py)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6167:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:70:| B2: compute_p0 silently computes on incomplete subset | **VETO** | 15b87fb: compute_p0.py strict-completeness — fails loudly on missing tags / duplicates / seed mismatch / N≠144. Denominator pinned to PREREG-frozen 144. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6168:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:83:| Q6.c: yellow-band rule for borderline p_0 | CHALLENGE (open) | Codex previously said "don't post-hoc retune" — accepted; no yellow band defined; passive accept on (0, 0.10] |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6170:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:101:- (RQ2.b) Strict-completeness failure modes call `sys.exit("ERROR: ...")`. The runner has `set +e` around compute_p0 invocation specifically (line ~245), captures EXIT, then propagates. Is exit code 1 (sys.exit with string) vs 2 (ceiling) handled distinctly?

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6411:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4135:./handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:248:                emit_synthetic_unsolved "$OUT_FILE" "$MODE" "$SEED" "$PID" "problem_file_missing" 99
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6439:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4489:I found one concrete mismatch with the stated round-2 invariant: the runner comments now say synthetic rows are used only for timeout, but the full loop still emits a synthetic UNSOLVED row when the MiniF2F problem file is missing. I’m checking whether that can pass strict completeness and bias `p_0`, because that would be the same class of silent data-loss problem as the prior VETO.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6441:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4664:   The runner claims synthetic rows are “USED ONLY” for timeout at [run_p0_calibration.sh:184](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:184), but the loop still emits a synthetic UNSOLVED row for `PROBLEM_NOT_FOUND` at [run_p0_calibration.sh:246](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:246). `compute_p0.py` only requires tags, seed set, matched problem sets, and row counts at [compute_p0.py:69](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:69), [compute_p0.py:93](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:93), [compute_p0.py:110](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:110), and [compute_p0.py:116](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:116). It does not reject `synthetic_reason=problem_file_missing`. I verified a 144-problem × 2-seed all-`problem_file_missing` synthetic dataset exits `0` with `p0=0.0`. That is the same silent data-loss class as the prior crash-as-data VETO.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6442:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4667:   The preflight runs `timeout 30 "$EVALUATOR" ... || true` at [run_p0_calibration.sh:141](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:141), then only greps output for `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED` or unexpected panic text at [run_p0_calibration.sh:142](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:142) and [run_p0_calibration.sh:150](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:150). A preflight timeout `124` or empty-output hang would print “OK” at [run_p0_calibration.sh:156](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:156). Since in-loop `124` becomes synthetic UNSOLVED at [run_p0_calibration.sh:285](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:285), this can still turn an evaluator-level infrastructure failure into apparently complete data.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6443:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4671:The specific round-2 VETO, `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED` panic absorbed as valid synthetic data, is resolved for in-loop evaluator crashes: only exit `124` emits synthetic data at [run_p0_calibration.sh:285](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:285); other exits abort at [run_p0_calibration.sh:300](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:300), with `TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED` mapped to exit `3` at [run_p0_calibration.sh:301](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:301). The evaluator now calls `verify_trust_root` before loading problems or making LLM calls at [evaluator.rs:176](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:176) and panics on failure at [evaluator.rs:181](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:181).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6444:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4673:B2/B3 are substantially fixed: strict completeness and denominator pinning are at [compute_p0.py:88](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:88) and [compute_p0.py:151](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:151); runner propagates estimator exit `2` as abort at [run_p0_calibration.sh:365](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:365).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6445:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4689:   The runner claims synthetic rows are “USED ONLY” for timeout at [run_p0_calibration.sh:184](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:184), but the loop still emits a synthetic UNSOLVED row for `PROBLEM_NOT_FOUND` at [run_p0_calibration.sh:246](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:246). `compute_p0.py` only requires tags, seed set, matched problem sets, and row counts at [compute_p0.py:69](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:69), [compute_p0.py:93](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:93), [compute_p0.py:110](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:110), and [compute_p0.py:116](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:116). It does not reject `synthetic_reason=problem_file_missing`. I verified a 144-problem × 2-seed all-`problem_file_missing` synthetic dataset exits `0` with `p0=0.0`. That is the same silent data-loss class as the prior crash-as-data VETO.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6453:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:80:- (Q3.e) Boltzmann seeds [31415, 2718] frozen in PREREG; `run_p0_calibration.sh` hardcodes them. If a future drift forces re-calibration, are the seeds in Trust Root manifest sufficient to detect tampering, or could a runner script tamper go undetected?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6456:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:106:- (Q6.a) Is exit-2 actually an ABORT in the runner workflow, or could a downstream script swallow it and continue? Test by inspection: where in the pipeline does compute_p0.py run, and what handles exit code?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6457:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:107:- (Q6.b) p_0 > 0.10 means treatment failed too aggressively. The mitigation per PREREG = "redesign rollback simulation, redo". Are we confident the synthetic-short-circuit-at-tx-50 design is FAR enough from the ceiling that we won't hit it? If most adaptation problems take >50 tx to solve, p_0 will be HIGH. Pre-batch evidence (smoke) doesn't tell us this.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6458:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:108:- (Q6.c) If p_0 is in (0.05, 0.10] — borderline — is that a passive accept, or should a borderline result trigger automatic re-calibration with a different threshold (e.g., tx 75) to verify p_0 is stable?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6460:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:115:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?

The runner has `set +e` around compute_p0 invocation specifically (line ~245), captures EXIT, then propagates. Is exit code 1 (sys.exit with string) vs 2 (ceiling) handled distinctly?

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6478:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:62:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6485:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:95:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6493:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:120:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. Acceptable uses: round-1/2/3 retrospective text describing what the packet USED to say.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6512:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:167:- **A1**: PREREG amendment p_0 calibration deferral + Trust Root 24 → 25.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6517:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:184:6be6eb4  A1:  PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24 → 25
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6525:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:219:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6540:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:290:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6551:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:369:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6557:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:437:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6628:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5139:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6635:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5146:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6637:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5191:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6880:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5409:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6884:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5416:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6885:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5417:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:92:Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:

No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6985:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5849:   109	6be6eb4  A1:  PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24 → 25
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6995:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5884:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7278:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144:6be6eb4  A1:  PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24 → 25
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7286:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:179:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7301:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:250:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7313:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:27:-   (Q2.a) The substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` is conservative and does not introduce Type-I inflation, as it holds artifacts to the strictest possible standard defined in the original PREREG.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7368:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:-   **(Q6.a) `exit 2` abort**: **CHALLENGE**. The runner script `run_p0_calibration.sh` does not use `set -e`. If `compute_p0.py` exits with code 2, the script will continue executing, printing the summary box. An automated runner could miss the failure. The script should use `set -e` or explicitly check the exit code of `compute_p0.py` and abort.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7369:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:65:-   **(Q6.b) `p_0 > 0.10` meaning**: **PASS**. The interpretation is correct. This is the intended behavior of the guardrail: if the system is too brittle or problems are too hard (requiring >50 tx), the calibration fails, preventing a potentially flawed `p_0` from being frozen.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9231:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:16:| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9232:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9233:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:46:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.

It remains the immutable round-4 frozen pre-registration. This amendment is a separate document referenced from § 5.5 forward via a pointer added to Trust Root manifest.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9335:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9342:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9840:   179	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9972:    10	- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10317:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., th-04-26.md:492:This protocol forecloses the round-2 tuning-surface attack: every parameter (toggle, sample, estimator, schedule, freeze point, ceiling) is pre-committed; the calibration runs are observable jsonl; the value enters Trust Root. There is no analyst degree of freedom left except the 0.10 ceiling, and the ceiling is itself an abort condition (high p_0 ⇒ design problem, not an opportunity to pass weaker artifacts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:505:Gate H: count of artifacts j with ArtifactState(j) = Certified ≥ 1
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:508:`Certified` requires j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR ALL to reject the null at the family-corrected α level (per § 5.2 + § 9). It is the conjunction of inferential outcomes, not a separate inferential test. Reaching Gate H = 1+ artifacts pass all four heldout tests after correction. H5 is therefore a deterministic OR over the artifact-level outcomes; no separate p-value.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:568:- E3 Statistical inference per § 5.2 + § 9. For each j, evaluate the four primary tests (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR). Holm-Bonferroni over the full family `4 + 4k`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:586:| H | **Heldout WBCG_PPUT > 0 with ArtifactState = Certified gate** (round 2 — RR criterion relaxed per Gemini audit M4): at least one ArchitectAI-generated user-space artifact Δ has `ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified` per § 1.7 — meaning (i) was Accepted (passed § 3 + § 3.5 AuditorAI battery), (ii) was Quarantined (user-approved + ΔPPUT_meta_val > 0 + N_use ≥ 3 on meta_val), (iii) at heldout sealed eval all FOUR primary inferential tests reject the null at family-corrected α (per § 5.2 + § 9): **j-PPUT** (ΔPPUT_heldout > 0 by paired binomial), **j-FAR** (ΔFAR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), **j-RR** (per-problem regression rate ≤ baseline `p_0` by exact one-sided binomial — NOT the brittle `RR = 0` from round 1), **j-CPR** (ΔCPR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), AND (iv) is rollbackable (artifact directory deletion at the protocol level restores prior heldout PPUT — verified by an explicit rollback sub-eval, see § 6 E2). Quarantined-only artifacts (failed promotion) do NOT count toward Gate H. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:619:**Phase E decisions** (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR for each Quarantined artifact j): same family of N_max = 44. The 4 Phase C tests + 4·k actual Phase E tests sort within the same Holm procedure.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:621:H5 (Gate H = at least one `Certified` artifact) is **NOT** a separate inferential test — it is a deterministic OR over per-artifact outcomes that already passed the family-corrected α (per § 5.4).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:682:| "TuringOS achieves capability compilation" | WBCG_PPUT > 0 on heldout (Gate H, § 7) | "on MiniF2F-Test heldout-54 with deepseek-v4-flash thinking-off + Phase D heterogeneous (v4-flash thinking-on + Gemini 2.5 Pro)" |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:697:| Phase D ≥ 1 Quarantined, Phase E zero Certified | "Phase D produced k Quarantined artifact(s) with positive ΔPPUT on meta_validation; on heldout-54 sealed eval, no artifact survived all four primary inferential tests (j-PPUT / j-FAR / j-RR / j-CPR) at family-corrected α; capability-compilation gain on Phase D set did not generalize to heldout under Holm-Bonferroni correction" |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:699:| Phase E sealed eval but rollback test fails | "FULL PASS NOT achieved — Gate H sub-criterion (rollbackable) did not hold; artifact effects entangled with adaptation state and could not be cleanly reverted; arc reported as infrastructure-built / capability-not-cleanly-attributed" |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:777:| 2026-04-26 | **Patch A** (Gemini DeepThink): § 1.7 ArtifactState 4-state machine for artifacts (Accepted / Quarantined / Certified / Reverted); § 1.6 WBCG_PPUT row + § 7 Gate H tightened to "Certified-only". Task Progress remains binary (Lean perfect predicate). | Gemini DeepThink 2026-04-26 PPUT-driven FULL PASS confirmation |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:783:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M1**: § 5 restructured — each H1-H4 has ONE primary endpoint (per-(problem,seed) Lean-verified Progress sign or paired VPPUT sign); H5 reclassified as deterministic gate (no α). § 5.2 added — per-artifact heldout family `4·k` tests (j-PPUT / j-FAR / j-RR / j-CPR). § 5.3 family size = `4 + 4k`. § 6 C2 independent unit clarified = (problem, seed). § 9 rewritten with Holm-Bonferroni stepwise procedure + power expectation + family construction frozen at A5. | Codex audit M1 + Gemini audit M1 (round 1 dual-CHALLENGE convergent) |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:786:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M4**: § 7 Gate H — RR criterion relaxed from brittle `RR = 0` to per-problem one-sided exact binomial test against pre-registered baseline regression rate `p_0` (calibrated in Phase B6/B7 on adaptation set). Reachability calibrated to empirical reality. | Gemini audit M4 (round 1 GATE-H REACHABILITY CHALLENGE — Codex missed) |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:794:| 2026-04-26 (round 3) | **R3-2**: § 5.2 p_0 calibration protocol fully specified — toggle (`--simulate-rollback-at-tx-50`), sample (full adaptation-144 × 2 seeds = 288 paired runs), estimator (per-problem worst-case across seeds), schedule (Phase B7 mandatory before Phase D), freeze (genesis_payload.toml + Trust Root SHA-256 of calibration jsonl), ceiling (p_0 > 0.10 = ABORT), audit (dual-audit at Phase B → C transition). Closes round-2 tuning-surface concern. | Codex round-2 audit P0-gate-h (partial) |

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:3924:Overall: **CHALLENGE**. The arc is not fundamentally broken, but the current PREREG has three preregistration-level ambiguities that peer review could exploit: heldout sealing is not actually sealed from agents, Gate H attribution is underdefined under a single heldout eval, and the statistical family/sample unit is not closed enough.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4025:**Mandate**: this is round 2. In round 1 you returned **CHALLENGE** with 3 P0 items: (M-leak) heldout sealing operational not cryptographic; (M-measure) Gate H per-artifact measurement underdefined under single sealed eval; (M-fam) statistical family closure. Gemini independently CHALLENGEd with 3 P0s of its own: (M-stat) per-artifact guardrail tests omitted from family; (M-gate-h) RR=0 brittleness; (M-spof) human-as-meta-predicate SPOF. Conservative merge → CHALLENGE.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4053:- Round-2 claim: § 7 Gate H, j-RR test relaxed to per-problem one-sided exact binomial vs pre-registered baseline regression rate `p_0` calibrated in Phase B6/B7 on adaptation set.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4054:- **Your check**: is `p_0` calibration protocol specified clearly enough that it cannot be tuned post-hoc to be artificially high (auto-passing test)? When is `p_0` frozen, and what's the audit trail?
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4068:- Any NEW issues introduced by round-2 revisions (e.g., Holm-Bonferroni dependence on k introducing analyst degree of freedom; LOO factorial assumption; baseline `p_0` calibration timing creating new attack surface)
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4167:| WBCG_PPUT | sum over candidate artifacts Δ of `1[ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified]` where `Certified` requires the conjunction of all four heldout primary inferential tests rejecting null at family-corrected α (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR per § 5.2) AND Rollbackable(Δ) = 1 (see § 1.7 for ArtifactState; § 7 Gate H for full reachability conditions) | Capability-compilation success |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4190:              > 0), j-FAR (non-inferiority), j-RR (binomial vs p_0), j-CPR
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4191:              (non-inferiority). Counts toward WBCG_PPUT (§ 1.6 + § 7 Gate H).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4397:**Final FULL PASS** = WBCG_PPUT > 0 on heldout (Gate H, § 7).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4422:| `j-RR` | Artifact j regression rate ≤ baseline `p_0` | One-sided exact binomial on count of regressions (heldout problems SOLVED at baseline UNSOLVED with j) vs pre-registered baseline rate `p_0` measured on adaptation pre-Phase-D | problem | RR_j ≤ p_0 |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4425:`p_0` is calibrated as a Phase B6 deliverable (Phase B7 if delayed): measured baseline regression rate when ablating any single recent code change on adaptation set. Pre-committed `p_0` value goes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` before Phase E starts. Phase E cannot start without `p_0` frozen.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4438:Gate H: count of artifacts j with ArtifactState(j) = Certified ≥ 1
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4441:`Certified` requires j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR ALL to reject the null at the family-corrected α level (per § 5.2 + § 9). It is the conjunction of inferential outcomes, not a separate inferential test. Reaching Gate H = 1+ artifacts pass all four heldout tests after correction. H5 is therefore a deterministic OR over the artifact-level outcomes; no separate p-value.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4501:- E3 Statistical inference per § 5.2 + § 9. For each j, evaluate the four primary tests (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR). Holm-Bonferroni over the full family `4 + 4k`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4519:| H | **Heldout WBCG_PPUT > 0 with ArtifactState = Certified gate** (round 2 — RR criterion relaxed per Gemini audit M4): at least one ArchitectAI-generated user-space artifact Δ has `ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified` per § 1.7 — meaning (i) was Accepted (passed § 3 + § 3.5 AuditorAI battery), (ii) was Quarantined (user-approved + ΔPPUT_meta_val > 0 + N_use ≥ 3 on meta_val), (iii) at heldout sealed eval all FOUR primary inferential tests reject the null at family-corrected α (per § 5.2 + § 9): **j-PPUT** (ΔPPUT_heldout > 0 by paired binomial), **j-FAR** (ΔFAR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), **j-RR** (per-problem regression rate ≤ baseline `p_0` by exact one-sided binomial — NOT the brittle `RR = 0` from round 1), **j-CPR** (ΔCPR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), AND (iv) is rollbackable (artifact directory deletion at the protocol level restores prior heldout PPUT — verified by an explicit rollback sub-eval, see § 6 E2). Quarantined-only artifacts (failed promotion) do NOT count toward Gate H. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4541:H5 (Gate H = at least one `Certified` artifact) is **NOT** a separate inferential test — it is a deterministic OR over per-artifact outcomes that already passed the family-corrected α (per § 5.4).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4588:| "TuringOS achieves capability compilation" | WBCG_PPUT > 0 on heldout (Gate H, § 7) | "on MiniF2F-Test heldout-54 with deepseek-v4-flash thinking-off + Phase D heterogeneous (v4-flash thinking-on + Gemini 2.5 Pro)" |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4602:| Phase D ≥ 1 Quarantined, Phase E zero Certified | "Phase D produced k Quarantined artifact(s) with positive ΔPPUT on meta_validation; on heldout-54 sealed eval, no artifact survived all four primary inferential tests (j-PPUT / j-FAR / j-RR / j-CPR) at family-corrected α; capability-compilation gain on Phase D set did not generalize to heldout under Holm-Bonferroni correction" |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4604:| Phase E sealed eval but rollback test fails | "FULL PASS NOT achieved — Gate H sub-criterion (rollbackable) did not hold; artifact effects entangled with adaptation state and could not be cleanly reverted; arc reported as infrastructure-built / capability-not-cleanly-attributed" |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4682:| 2026-04-26 | **Patch A** (Gemini DeepThink): § 1.7 ArtifactState 4-state machine for artifacts (Accepted / Quarantined / Certified / Reverted); § 1.6 WBCG_PPUT row + § 7 Gate H tightened to "Certified-only". Task Progress remains binary (Lean perfect predicate). | Gemini DeepThink 2026-04-26 PPUT-driven FULL PASS confirmation |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4688:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M1**: § 5 restructured — each H1-H4 has ONE primary endpoint (per-(problem,seed) Lean-verified Progress sign or paired VPPUT sign); H5 reclassified as deterministic gate (no α). § 5.2 added — per-artifact heldout family `4·k` tests (j-PPUT / j-FAR / j-RR / j-CPR). § 5.3 family size = `4 + 4k`. § 6 C2 independent unit clarified = (problem, seed). § 9 rewritten with Holm-Bonferroni stepwise procedure + power expectation + family construction frozen at A5. | Codex audit M1 + Gemini audit M1 (round 1 dual-CHALLENGE convergent) |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4691:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M4**: § 7 Gate H — RR criterion relaxed from brittle `RR = 0` to per-problem one-sided exact binomial test against pre-registered baseline regression rate `p_0` (calibrated in Phase B6/B7 on adaptation set). Reachability calibrated to empirical reality. | Gemini audit M4 (round 1 GATE-H REACHABILITY CHALLENGE — Codex missed) |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:4883:(Pre-reg restates Gate H as the WBCG_PPUT > 0 success criterion + folds the architect's "PPUT-metric-shielding" into the anti-Goodhart conformance battery, since shielding is enforcement-side and success is measurement-side.
