handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8203:Overall: **CHALLENGE**. The arc is not fundamentally broken, but the current PREREG has three preregistration-level ambiguities that peer review could exploit: heldout sealing is not actually sealed from agents, Gate H attribution is underdefined under a single heldout eval, and the statistical family/sample unit is not closed enough.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8222:2. **Define Gate H measurement (§1.7, §6 E2-E3, §7 H):** specify per-seed aggregation, baseline/post protocol, per-artifact attribution, rollback test, and when `Certified` is minted, all within the “single sealed heldout eval” constraint.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8241:Overall: **CHALLENGE**. The arc is not fundamentally broken, but the current PREREG has three preregistration-level ambiguities that peer review could exploit: heldout sealing is not actually sealed from agents, Gate H attribution is underdefined under a single heldout eval, and the statistical family/sample unit is not closed enough.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8260:2. **Define Gate H measurement (§1.7, §6 E2-E3, §7 H):** specify per-seed aggregation, baseline/post protocol, per-artifact attribution, rollback test, and when `Certified` is minted, all within the “single sealed heldout eval” constraint.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8293:| **P0-stat-gemini** | **partial** | §5.2 now includes per-artifact j-PPUT/j-FAR/j-RR/j-CPR in the family, but the same independence and dynamic-k/Gate-C timing defects mean family closure is not yet statistically clean. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8294:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §7 replaces brittle `RR=0` with an exact binomial test against `p_0`, improving reachability, but §5.2 defines `p_0` as “ablating any single recent code change” without fixing the code-change set, estimator, seeds, sample, or freeze point before artifact generation; this can be tuned high enough to auto-pass. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8303:2. **RR baseline `p_0` is a new tuning surface**: §5.2 must specify the exact Phase B calibration protocol and freeze `p_0` before Phase D artifact generation, with an immutable audit trail.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8319:| **P0-stat-gemini** | **partial** | §5.2 now includes per-artifact j-PPUT/j-FAR/j-RR/j-CPR in the family, but the same independence and dynamic-k/Gate-C timing defects mean family closure is not yet statistically clean. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8320:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §7 replaces brittle `RR=0` with an exact binomial test against `p_0`, improving reachability, but §5.2 defines `p_0` as “ablating any single recent code change” without fixing the code-change set, estimator, seeds, sample, or freeze point before artifact generation; this can be tuned high enough to auto-pass. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8329:2. **RR baseline `p_0` is a new tuning surface**: §5.2 must specify the exact Phase B calibration protocol and freeze `p_0` before Phase D artifact generation, with an immutable audit trail.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8358:**closed** — The revised Gate H in § 7 replaces the brittle `RR=0` rule with a one-sided exact binomial test against a baseline `p_0` that § 5.2 requires to be pre-calibrated and frozen before Phase E.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8368:### P0-measure (Codex axis 5, Gate H per-artifact measurement)
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8392:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §5.2 now fully specifies and freezes `p_0`, closing the tuning-surface issue, but the new `p_0 ≤ 0.10` exact-binomial criterion appears mathematically unreachable for j-RR under §7 + §9 Holm thresholds: even 0/54 regressions gives best-case p `0.9^54 ≈ 0.0034`, above early `N_max=44` thresholds. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8401:2. **j-RR certification is likely impossible as written**: §5.2 caps `p_0` at 0.10, while §7 requires j-RR to reject at family-corrected α. With heldout n=54, zero regressions still cannot clear early Holm thresholds under `N_max=44`. If RR is meant as a guardrail non-rejection instead, §1.6/§7 must stop saying all four tests reject.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8417:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §5.2 now fully specifies and freezes `p_0`, closing the tuning-surface issue, but the new `p_0 ≤ 0.10` exact-binomial criterion appears mathematically unreachable for j-RR under §7 + §9 Holm thresholds: even 0/54 regressions gives best-case p `0.9^54 ≈ 0.0034`, above early `N_max=44` thresholds. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8426:2. **j-RR certification is likely impossible as written**: §5.2 caps `p_0` at 0.10, while §7 requires j-RR to reject at family-corrected α. With heldout n=54, zero regressions still cannot clear early Holm thresholds under `N_max=44`. If RR is meant as a guardrail non-rejection instead, §1.6/§7 must stop saying all four tests reject.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3491:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:36:-   **(RQ2.a) Synthetic Row Correctness:** The `emit_synthetic_unsolved` function in `run_p0_calibration.sh` correctly generates a JSONL row with `"solved": false`, `"progress": 0`, and `"synthetic_timeout_or_crash": true`. The `solved()` function in `compute_p0.py` correctly interprets this as UNSOLVED by checking `int(row["progress"]) == 1`. The smoke test evidence confirms the treatment run produced a row with `solved: false` and `synthetic_short_circuit: true`, which is handled by the same logic.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3504:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:78:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3507:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:115:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?

The `solved()` function in `compute_p0.py` correctly interprets this as UNSOLVED by checking `int(row["progress"]) == 1`. The smoke test evidence confirms the treatment run produced a row with `solved: false` and `synthetic_short_circuit: true`, which is handled by the same logic.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3883:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5479:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:85:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3884:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5531:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1297:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3885:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5583:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3283:    64	> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3961:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7329:- Synthetic rows include every required v2 `RunAggregate` field from [jsonl_schema.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs:82); emitted fields are at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:231). `synthetic_short_circuit` being absent is not a conflict.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3962:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7330:- `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` / `BUILD_SHA` are adequate for this batch: stamped in runner at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:68), read by evaluator at [evaluator.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1216).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3963:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7362:- Synthetic rows include every required v2 `RunAggregate` field from [jsonl_schema.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs:82); emitted fields are at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:231). `synthetic_short_circuit` being absent is not a conflict.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3964:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7363:- `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` / `BUILD_SHA` are adequate for this batch: stamped in runner at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:68), read by evaluator at [evaluator.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1216).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4198:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED

For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4205:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:122:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4222:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1270:2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4226:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1297:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4228:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1309:Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4229:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1311:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4260:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3256:    37	2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4264:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3283:    64	> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4266:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3295:    76	Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4267:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3297:    78	Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.

For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4327:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3855:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1270:2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4331:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3860:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1297:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4333:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3862:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1311:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4419:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:64:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4421:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:76:Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7520:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:119:2. ~~**TRACE_MATRIX of B7-extra (p_0 calibration toggle)**~~ — landed. Final implementation differs slightly from the original sketch in this section: the constitutional `bus.register_predicate(...)` API does not currently exist on `main` (it lives on the unmerged `phase-z-wtool-tools` branch — TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC1-N11 references it aspirationally). Rather than scope-creep B7-extra into reviving Phase Z, the synthetic predicate is implemented at the evaluator layer in `rollback_sim.rs` with an explicit short-circuit at the threshold tx. The constitutional anchor (FC1-E18 ∏p=0 → Q_t repeated, then FC2-N22 HALT via existing `MaxTxExhausted`) is unchanged; only the abstraction depth differs. Listed under § 2 above as ✅ entries.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:8628:3. **Model canonicalization is incomplete and already violated.** PREREG pins `deepseek-v4-flash` and says both `deepseek-chat` and `deepseek-reasoner` are deprecated aliases ([PREREG:705](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:705), [PREREG:706](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:706)). R-019 only warns on `deepseek-chat` ([R-019:9](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/rules/active/R-019_model_snapshot_canonical.yaml:9)), while the production evaluator defaults to `deepseek-reasoner` ([evaluator.rs:203](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:203)) and the Trust Root calibration runner defaults to `deepseek-chat` ([run_p0_calibration.sh:43](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:43)). `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` then inherits that alias if unset ([evaluator.rs:1216](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1216)).

handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:28:-   **(Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording**: **PASS**.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:29:    -   `handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`: § 2 and § 8 are now internally consistent. § 2 correctly identifies `p_0 = 0.10` as the "LEAST strict admissible ceiling" and explains that a smaller `p_0` is stricter. The statistical implications regarding Type-I inflation are correctly stated. This resolves the substantive logical contradictions found in rounds 1, 7, and 8 (fixes F6, M4, N1).
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:31:    -   `handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`: § 3 lists the conditions "for re-calibration". § 2 and § 8 clarify that these are pre-requisites and do not guarantee completion before any specific phase, stating that "Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution" if the conditions are not met. This framing is sound and avoids the over-promise caught in round 7.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:33:    -   `genesis_payload.toml`: The manifest correctly lists `"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md"`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:45:-   **(G2) PREREG Amendment Wording**: **VERIFIED**. A full-text search of `handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` confirms that all references to the `p_0=0.10` substitution are now consistent with it being the "least-strict admissible ceiling," not the "strictest." The correction note in § 2 and the re-worded audit requirement in § 8 are both clear and correct.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:21:### 2. R3-2 (p_0 protocol)
handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:23:The round 2 design introduced `p_0` (baseline regression rate) but left its calibration protocol underspecified, creating a new tuning surface as Codex correctly identified.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:31:- A `ceiling` (`p_0 > 0.10` is an abort condition, preventing gaming).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:28:**Mandate**: Phase A is pre-flight (days 1–3 of the 30-day arc). 8 atoms (A0a–e + A1–A7) must be auditable as a unit before Phase B (kernel instrumentation + PPUT accounting) is authorized to start. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md (round-4 PASS/PASS, frozen) + PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md (Trust Root entry 25) are the contracts you're auditing against.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:43:**Arc**: PPUT-CCL (`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` round-4 PASS/PASS + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:60:- **A1**: PREREG amendment p_0 calibration deferral.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:100:- File: `handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:101:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:102:- **Statistical framing**: `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows. `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. The substitution is operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; if/when § 3 re-calibration conditions complete, calibration upgrades the bar.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:159:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → **DEFERRED with substitution per amendment § 2**: `p_0 = 0.10` hardcoded at every Gate H consumer.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:162:- ✅ Tools qualified per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification): `runner.sh`, `compute_p0.py`, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:183:- (Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording: is `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 + § 8 internally consistent + statistically correct (least-strict admissible; no Type-I inflation; substitution operative at any phase including Phase E)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:222:**Constitutional pattern**: mirrors `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; mirrors `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` (frozen) + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` (delta). Per C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the gate machinery itself must be qualifiable; this history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:245:5. (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` calls `p_0=0.10` substitution "strictest possible substitute" — backwards (0.10 is least-strict admissible for `j-RR ≤ p_0`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:256:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 reworded: `p_0=0.10` → least-strict admissible ceiling; explicit Type-I implications paragraph added. Closes #5.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:439:1. (**Codex R8#1 — substantive**) PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8's audit-requirements text still contains the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Logical contradiction with the post-M4 § 2 + § 3 framing.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:518:## handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:521:# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:531:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:543:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:545:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:547:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:550:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:551:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:552:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:554:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:562:1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:570:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:576:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:579:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:582:- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:585:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:591:- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:605:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:615:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5512:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5518:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5520:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5526:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5571:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5900:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5902:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:18:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5908:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:24:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:6645:    62	- **Statistical framing**: `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows. `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7093:11:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7095:23:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7096:25:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7097:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7098:30:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7099:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7100:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7101:34:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7106:50:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7107:56:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7108:59:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7109:65:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7111:71:- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7112:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7763:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7766:    30	- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7767:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7768:    32	- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7770:    34	**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7778:    42	1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7786:    50	5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7792:    56	PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7795:    59	- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7798:    62	- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7801:    65	Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7821:    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:7831:    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:8526:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:222:1. Logical contradiction with the post-M4 § 2 + § 3 framing.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:8552:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:3:**Arc**: PPUT-CCL (`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` round-4 PASS/PASS + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:8596:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:18:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:9459:handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:418:**Why this changed**: Round 3 placed j-RR in the inferential family with H0: `regression_rate ≥ p_0 = 0.10`. Codex round-3 noted this is mathematically unwinnable: best case 0/54 regressions gives `p = 0.9^54 ≈ 0.00343`, but smallest Holm threshold at N_max = 44 was 0.05/44 ≈ 0.00114 → fails to reject even with zero regressions. Gate H unreachable.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:9461:handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:549:- E3 Statistical inference per § 5.3 + § 9. For each j, evaluate the **three** primary inferential tests (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-CPR) under Holm-Bonferroni at family `4 + 3k`, N_max = 34. j-RR is a separate descriptive guardrail check (point estimate ≤ p_0 per § 5.4), NOT in the inferential family.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:9473:handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:781:| 2026-04-26 (round 4) | **R4-2**: § 5.4 j-RR moved out of inferential family (was mathematically unwinnable: 0.9^54 ≈ 0.00343 > 0.05/44 ≈ 0.00114; even zero regressions failed to reject). Now a descriptive guardrail: point check `RR_j ≤ p_0`, no α correction. Family size shrinks from 4+4k to 4+3k; N_max from 44 to 34. § 1.6 + § 1.7 + § 7 Gate H synced to "3 inferential + 1 guardrail + rollback" framing. | Codex round-3 audit P0-gate-h (partial; mathematical impossibility) |

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:425:`p_0` is calibrated as a Phase B6 deliverable (Phase B7 if delayed): measured baseline regression rate when ablating any single recent code change on adaptation set. handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:438:Gate H: count of artifacts j with ArtifactState(j) = Certified ≥ 1
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:441:`Certified` requires j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR ALL to reject the null at the family-corrected α level (per § 5.2 + § 9). It is the conjunction of inferential outcomes, not a separate inferential test. Reaching Gate H = 1+ artifacts pass all four heldout tests after correction. H5 is therefore a deterministic OR over the artifact-level outcomes; no separate p-value.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:501:- E3 Statistical inference per § 5.2 + § 9. For each j, evaluate the four primary tests (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR). Holm-Bonferroni over the full family `4 + 4k`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:519:| H | **Heldout WBCG_PPUT > 0 with ArtifactState = Certified gate** (round 2 — RR criterion relaxed per Gemini audit M4): at least one ArchitectAI-generated user-space artifact Δ has `ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified` per § 1.7 — meaning (i) was Accepted (passed § 3 + § 3.5 AuditorAI battery), (ii) was Quarantined (user-approved + ΔPPUT_meta_val > 0 + N_use ≥ 3 on meta_val), (iii) at heldout sealed eval all FOUR primary inferential tests reject the null at family-corrected α (per § 5.2 + § 9): **j-PPUT** (ΔPPUT_heldout > 0 by paired binomial), **j-FAR** (ΔFAR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), **j-RR** (per-problem regression rate ≤ baseline `p_0` by exact one-sided binomial — NOT the brittle `RR = 0` from round 1), **j-CPR** (ΔCPR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), AND (iv) is rollbackable (artifact directory deletion at the protocol level restores prior heldout PPUT — verified by an explicit rollback sub-eval, see § 6 E2). Quarantined-only artifacts (failed promotion) do NOT count toward Gate H. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:541:H5 (Gate H = at least one `Certified` artifact) is **NOT** a separate inferential test — it is a deterministic OR over per-artifact outcomes that already passed the family-corrected α (per § 5.4).

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:4203:Overall: **CHALLENGE**. The arc is not fundamentally broken, but the current PREREG has three preregistration-level ambiguities that peer review could exploit: heldout sealing is not actually sealed from agents, Gate H attribution is underdefined under a single heldout eval, and the statistical family/sample unit is not closed enough.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:28:**Mandate**: Phase A is pre-flight (days 1–3 of the 30-day arc). 8 atoms (A0a–e + A1–A7) must be auditable as a unit before Phase B (kernel instrumentation + PPUT accounting) is authorized to start. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md (round-4 PASS/PASS, frozen) + PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md (Trust Root entry 25) are the contracts you're auditing against.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:60:- **A1**: PREREG amendment p_0 calibration deferral.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:100:- File: `handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:101:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:102:- **Statistical framing**: `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows. `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. The substitution is operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; if/when § 3 re-calibration conditions complete, calibration upgrades the bar.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:159:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → **DEFERRED with substitution per amendment § 2**: `p_0 = 0.10` hardcoded at every Gate H consumer.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:162:- ✅ Tools qualified per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification): `runner.sh`, `compute_p0.py`, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:183:- (Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording: is `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 + § 8 internally consistent + statistically correct (least-strict admissible; no Type-I inflation; substitution operative at any phase including Phase E)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:222:**Constitutional pattern**: mirrors `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; mirrors `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` (frozen) + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` (delta). Per C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the gate machinery itself must be qualifiable; this history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:245:5. (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` calls `p_0=0.10` substitution "strictest possible substitute" — backwards (0.10 is least-strict admissible for `j-RR ≤ p_0`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:256:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 reworded: `p_0=0.10` → least-strict admissible ceiling; explicit Type-I implications paragraph added. Closes #5.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:439:1. (**Codex R8#1 — substantive**) PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8's audit-requirements text still contains the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Logical contradiction with the post-M4 § 2 + § 3 framing.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:495:## handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:498:# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:508:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:520:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:522:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:524:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:527:- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:528:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:529:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:531:**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:539:1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:547:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:553:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:556:- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:559:- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:562:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:568:- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:582:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:592:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5450:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5454:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5456:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5462:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5507:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound.
