handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5801:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:23:PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5802:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5803:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5804:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5805:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:50:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5806:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:63:- ✅ Trust Root verifies clean
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5807:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:65:Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5808:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:70:- **PREREG § 1.8 Trust Root composition** — manifest entries unchanged by this amendment (this amendment doc is added per § 7 below).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5809:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:77:`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` itself is **NOT EDITED** by this amendment. It remains the immutable round-4 frozen pre-registration. This amendment is a separate document referenced from § 5.5 forward via a pointer added to Trust Root manifest.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5810:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:79:This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5811:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:81:## § 7. Trust Root impact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5812:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:85:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5813:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:92:Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5814:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5815:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:96:- Does deferral leave any phase blocked indefinitely? (Should not — § 3 lists explicit re-calibration conditions; if those never met, Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution per § 2 final paragraph. Calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete; absence of calibration is not a Phase E blocker.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5816:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:105:- `handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md` (PASS verdict on round-4 batch — but batch was 3-7 days not 8h, motivating this deferral)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5818:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5822:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5824:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e7 (post-A8e6, STRUCTURAL REWRITE per ultrathink constitutional alignment): rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document — every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Single-point fixes could not converge. **Resolution**: split the packet using the project's existing constitutional pattern (cf. `constitution.md` + Art. V.3 amendment log; `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` + `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md`; `TRACE_MATRIX_v0` + v1 + v2). New companion doc `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` carries the append-only chronology of rounds 1–6 + their in-cycle fix bundles. The packet itself becomes the canonical current-state snapshot — no round-N retrospectives, no fix-shipped sections, no cross-round staleness sources. FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance artifact restructure) + Art. V.1.2 (ArchitectAI commit authority on non-constitution files). Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification) the new history doc is in Trust Root so the chronology cannot be silently rewritten. Trust Root manifest 34 → 35 (`A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` added). Round 7 dual audit launches from this state.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5830:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:22:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6604:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6869:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6399:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:50:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6924:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6927:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:357:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.

No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7030:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:524:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7042:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5430:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7082:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6245:    50	5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7100:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7474:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:95:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7103:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7477:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:120:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. Acceptable uses: round-1/2/3 retrospective text describing what the packet USED to say.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7106:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7480:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7113:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7487:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7114:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7488:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:338:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7115:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7489:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:392:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7116:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7490:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7262:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7636:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7278:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7652:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5515:    55	- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.

(Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7344:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7718:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6762:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:55:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7345:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7719:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6766:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:80:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. Acceptable uses: round-1/2/3 retrospective text describing what the packet USED to say.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7347:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7721:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6768:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7355:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7729:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6855:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7371:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7745:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:7898:    16	- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7394:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7768:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8142:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7398:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7772:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8146:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7451:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8064:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:475:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7452:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8065:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:479:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7453:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8066:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:480:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7454:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8067:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:533:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7455:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8068:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:543:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7600:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8213:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5401:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).

FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7624:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8237:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5723:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7628:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8241:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5741:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7629:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8242:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5742:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7645:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8258:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5827:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7646:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8259:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5829:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7647:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8260:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5830:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7648:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8261:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5835:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7770:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8501:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:107:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7798:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8529:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:452:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7799:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8530:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:456:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7943:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8674:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5269:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7993:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8724:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6381:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7994:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8725:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6383:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:67:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7995:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8726:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6385:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7996:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8727:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6387:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8407:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6637:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5191:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |

No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8469:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6713:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:132:- **A1**: PREREG amendment p_0 calibration deferral + Trust Root 24 → 25.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8474:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6718:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:149:6be6eb4  A1:  PREREG amendment defer p_0 calibration + Trust Root 24 → 25
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8482:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6726:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8497:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6741:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:255:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8508:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6752:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8514:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6758:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8585:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6829:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5104:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 25 → 26.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8590:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6834:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8592:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6836:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5154:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |

Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8751:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6995:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5884:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. No further verification needed.

No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8828:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7072:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:6381:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

Audit packet should specifically test:

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8901:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7145:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7657:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 25 → 26.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8905:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7149:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7661:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9478:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:7525:   The frozen amendment is now correct: it says `0.10` is the least-strict admissible ceiling and may be less protective than calibrated `p_0` (`handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27-32`, `:95`). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9571:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5454:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9771:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:251:**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** as the conservative upper bound. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic; setting `p_0 = 0.10` (the maximum tolerated value) is the strictest possible substitute when no calibrated tighter value exists. This is mathematically conservative: artifacts must clear the strictest plausible bar, not a narrower data-derived bar.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12643:     1	# PREREG Amendment — p_0 Calibration Deferral (2026-04-25)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12653:    11	PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12665:    23	PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12667:    25	**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12669:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12672:    30	- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12673:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12674:    32	- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12676:    34	**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12821:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).

Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase".
