handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:6200:(Pre-reg restates Gate H as the WBCG_PPUT > 0 success criterion + folds the architect's "PPUT-metric-shielding" into the anti-Goodhart conformance battery, since shielding is enforcement-side and success is measurement-side. Both must hold for FULL PASS.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8203:Overall: **CHALLENGE**. The arc is not fundamentally broken, but the current PREREG has three preregistration-level ambiguities that peer review could exploit: heldout sealing is not actually sealed from agents, Gate H attribution is underdefined under a single heldout eval, and the statistical family/sample unit is not closed enough.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8222:2. **Define Gate H measurement (§1.7, §6 E2-E3, §7 H):** specify per-seed aggregation, baseline/post protocol, per-artifact attribution, rollback test, and when `Certified` is minted, all within the “single sealed heldout eval” constraint.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8241:Overall: **CHALLENGE**. The arc is not fundamentally broken, but the current PREREG has three preregistration-level ambiguities that peer review could exploit: heldout sealing is not actually sealed from agents, Gate H attribution is underdefined under a single heldout eval, and the statistical family/sample unit is not closed enough.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8260:2. **Define Gate H measurement (§1.7, §6 E2-E3, §7 H):** specify per-seed aggregation, baseline/post protocol, per-artifact attribution, rollback test, and when `Certified` is minted, all within the “single sealed heldout eval” constraint.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8293:| **P0-stat-gemini** | **partial** | §5.2 now includes per-artifact j-PPUT/j-FAR/j-RR/j-CPR in the family, but the same independence and dynamic-k/Gate-C timing defects mean family closure is not yet statistically clean. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8294:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §7 replaces brittle `RR=0` with an exact binomial test against `p_0`, improving reachability, but §5.2 defines `p_0` as “ablating any single recent code change” without fixing the code-change set, estimator, seeds, sample, or freeze point before artifact generation; this can be tuned high enough to auto-pass. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8303:2. **RR baseline `p_0` is a new tuning surface**: §5.2 must specify the exact Phase B calibration protocol and freeze `p_0` before Phase D artifact generation, with an immutable audit trail.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8319:| **P0-stat-gemini** | **partial** | §5.2 now includes per-artifact j-PPUT/j-FAR/j-RR/j-CPR in the family, but the same independence and dynamic-k/Gate-C timing defects mean family closure is not yet statistically clean. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8320:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §7 replaces brittle `RR=0` with an exact binomial test against `p_0`, improving reachability, but §5.2 defines `p_0` as “ablating any single recent code change” without fixing the code-change set, estimator, seeds, sample, or freeze point before artifact generation; this can be tuned high enough to auto-pass. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8329:2. **RR baseline `p_0` is a new tuning surface**: §5.2 must specify the exact Phase B calibration protocol and freeze `p_0` before Phase D artifact generation, with an immutable audit trail.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8358:**closed** — The revised Gate H in § 7 replaces the brittle `RR=0` rule with a one-sided exact binomial test against a baseline `p_0` that § 5.2 requires to be pre-calibrated and frozen before Phase E.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8368:### P0-measure (Codex axis 5, Gate H per-artifact measurement)
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8392:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §5.2 now fully specifies and freezes `p_0`, closing the tuning-surface issue, but the new `p_0 ≤ 0.10` exact-binomial criterion appears mathematically unreachable for j-RR under §7 + §9 Holm thresholds: even 0/54 regressions gives best-case p `0.9^54 ≈ 0.0034`, above early `N_max=44` thresholds. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8401:2. **j-RR certification is likely impossible as written**: §5.2 caps `p_0` at 0.10, while §7 requires j-RR to reject at family-corrected α. With heldout n=54, zero regressions still cannot clear early Holm thresholds under `N_max=44`. If RR is meant as a guardrail non-rejection instead, §1.6/§7 must stop saying all four tests reject.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8417:| **P0-gate-h** | **partial** | §5.2 now fully specifies and freezes `p_0`, closing the tuning-surface issue, but the new `p_0 ≤ 0.10` exact-binomial criterion appears mathematically unreachable for j-RR under §7 + §9 Holm thresholds: even 0/54 regressions gives best-case p `0.9^54 ≈ 0.0034`, above early `N_max=44` thresholds. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:8426:2. **j-RR certification is likely impossible as written**: §5.2 caps `p_0` at 0.10, while §7 requires j-RR to reject at family-corrected α. With heldout n=54, zero regressions still cannot clear early Holm thresholds under `N_max=44`. If RR is meant as a guardrail non-rejection instead, §1.6/§7 must stop saying all four tests reject.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3491:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:36:-   **(RQ2.a) Synthetic Row Correctness:** The `emit_synthetic_unsolved` function in `run_p0_calibration.sh` correctly generates a JSONL row with `"solved": false`, `"progress": 0`, and `"synthetic_timeout_or_crash": true`. The `solved()` function in `compute_p0.py` correctly interprets this as UNSOLVED by checking `int(row["progress"]) == 1`. The smoke test evidence confirms the treatment run produced a row with `solved: false` and `synthetic_short_circuit: true`, which is handled by the same logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3500:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:40:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3504:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:78:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3507:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:115:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3850:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4888:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:36:-   **(RQ2.a) Synthetic Row Correctness:** The `emit_synthetic_unsolved` function in `run_p0_calibration.sh` correctly generates a JSONL row with `"solved": false`, `"progress": 0`, and `"synthetic_timeout_or_crash": true`. The `solved()` function in `compute_p0.py` correctly interprets this as UNSOLVED by checking `int(row["progress"]) == 1`. The smoke test evidence confirms the treatment run produced a row with `solved: false` and `synthetic_short_circuit: true`, which is handled by the same logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3851:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4895:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:40:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3852:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4897:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:78:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?

For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3884:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5531:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1297:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3899:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5692:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3923:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3608:handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:37:export ACTIVE_MODEL="${ACTIVE_MODEL:-deepseek-chat}"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3900:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5711:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4123:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:64:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3901:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5819:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:64:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3902:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5885:./handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:406:    head -1 "${OUT_PREFIX}_control.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; r=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); print(' control:', {k: r.get(k) for k in ('tx_count','solved','progress','synthetic_short_circuit','synthetic_timeout_or_crash')})" || true
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3903:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5886:./handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:407:    head -1 "${OUT_PREFIX}_treatment.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; r=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); print(' treatment:', {k: r.get(k) for k in ('tx_count','solved','progress','synthetic_short_circuit','synthetic_timeout_or_crash')})" || true
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3961:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7329:- Synthetic rows include every required v2 `RunAggregate` field from [jsonl_schema.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs:82); emitted fields are at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:231). `synthetic_short_circuit` being absent is not a conflict.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3962:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7330:- `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` / `BUILD_SHA` are adequate for this batch: stamped in runner at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:68), read by evaluator at [evaluator.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1216).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3963:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7362:- Synthetic rows include every required v2 `RunAggregate` field from [jsonl_schema.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs:82); emitted fields are at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:231). `synthetic_short_circuit` being absent is not a conflict.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3964:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7363:- `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` / `BUILD_SHA` are adequate for this batch: stamped in runner at [run_p0_calibration.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:68), read by evaluator at [evaluator.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1216).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4198:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4202:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:85:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4205:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:122:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4222:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1270:2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4226:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1297:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4228:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1309:Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4229:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1311:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4260:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3256:    37	2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4264:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3283:    64	> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4266:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3295:    76	Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4396:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4619:handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:78:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4415:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:37:2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4419:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:64:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4421:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:76:Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4422:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:78:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7520:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:119:2. ~~**TRACE_MATRIX of B7-extra (p_0 calibration toggle)**~~ — landed. Final implementation differs slightly from the original sketch in this section: the constitutional `bus.register_predicate(...)` API does not currently exist on `main` (it lives on the unmerged `phase-z-wtool-tools` branch — TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC1-N11 references it aspirationally). Rather than scope-creep B7-extra into reviving Phase Z, the synthetic predicate is implemented at the evaluator layer in `rollback_sim.rs` with an explicit short-circuit at the threshold tx. The constitutional anchor (FC1-E18 ∏p=0 → Q_t repeated, then FC2-N22 HALT via existing `MaxTxExhausted`) is unchanged; only the abstraction depth differs. Listed under § 2 above as ✅ entries.

handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:23:The round 2 design introduced `p_0` (baseline regression rate) but left its calibration protocol underspecified, creating a new tuning surface as Codex correctly identified.

handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py:62:**Mandate**: Phase A is pre-flight (days 1–3 of the 30-day arc). 8 atoms (A0a–e + A1–A7) must be auditable as a unit before Phase B (kernel instrumentation + PPUT accounting) is authorized to start. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md (round-4 PASS/PASS, frozen) + PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md (Trust Root entry 25) are the contracts.
handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py:88:    "handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md",
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:25:**Mandate**: this is round 2. In round 1 you returned **CHALLENGE** with 3 P0 items: (M-leak) heldout sealing operational not cryptographic; (M-measure) Gate H per-artifact measurement underdefined under single sealed eval; (M-fam) statistical family closure. Gemini independently CHALLENGEd with 3 P0s of its own: (M-stat) per-artifact guardrail tests omitted from family; (M-gate-h) RR=0 brittleness; (M-spof) human-as-meta-predicate SPOF. Conservative merge → CHALLENGE.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:53:- Round-2 claim: § 7 Gate H, j-RR test relaxed to per-problem one-sided exact binomial vs pre-registered baseline regression rate `p_0` calibrated in Phase B6/B7 on adaptation set.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:167:| WBCG_PPUT | sum over candidate artifacts Δ of `1[ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified]` where `Certified` requires the conjunction of all four heldout primary inferential tests rejecting null at family-corrected α (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR per § 5.2) AND Rollbackable(Δ) = 1 (see § 1.7 for ArtifactState; § 7 Gate H for full reachability conditions) | Capability-compilation success |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:190:              > 0), j-FAR (non-inferiority), j-RR (binomial vs p_0), j-CPR
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:191:              (non-inferiority). Counts toward WBCG_PPUT (§ 1.6 + § 7 Gate H).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:397:**Final FULL PASS** = WBCG_PPUT > 0 on heldout (Gate H, § 7).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:422:| `j-RR` | Artifact j regression rate ≤ baseline `p_0` | One-sided exact binomial on count of regressions (heldout problems SOLVED at baseline UNSOLVED with j) vs pre-registered baseline rate `p_0` measured on adaptation pre-Phase-D | problem | RR_j ≤ p_0 |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:425:`p_0` is calibrated as a Phase B6 deliverable (Phase B7 if delayed): measured baseline regression rate when ablating any single recent code change on adaptation set. Pre-committed `p_0` value goes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` before Phase E starts. Phase E cannot start without `p_0` frozen.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:438:Gate H: count of artifacts j with ArtifactState(j) = Certified ≥ 1
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:441:`Certified` requires j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR ALL to reject the null at the family-corrected α level (per § 5.2 + § 9). It is the conjunction of inferential outcomes, not a separate inferential test. Reaching Gate H = 1+ artifacts pass all four heldout tests after correction. H5 is therefore a deterministic OR over the artifact-level outcomes; no separate p-value.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:501:- E3 Statistical inference per § 5.2 + § 9. For each j, evaluate the four primary tests (j-PPUT, j-FAR, j-RR, j-CPR). Holm-Bonferroni over the full family `4 + 4k`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:519:| H | **Heldout WBCG_PPUT > 0 with ArtifactState = Certified gate** (round 2 — RR criterion relaxed per Gemini audit M4): at least one ArchitectAI-generated user-space artifact Δ has `ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified` per § 1.7 — meaning (i) was Accepted (passed § 3 + § 3.5 AuditorAI battery), (ii) was Quarantined (user-approved + ΔPPUT_meta_val > 0 + N_use ≥ 3 on meta_val), (iii) at heldout sealed eval all FOUR primary inferential tests reject the null at family-corrected α (per § 5.2 + § 9): **j-PPUT** (ΔPPUT_heldout > 0 by paired binomial), **j-FAR** (ΔFAR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), **j-RR** (per-problem regression rate ≤ baseline `p_0` by exact one-sided binomial — NOT the brittle `RR = 0` from round 1), **j-CPR** (ΔCPR ≤ 0 by non-inferiority), AND (iv) is rollbackable (artifact directory deletion at the protocol level restores prior heldout PPUT — verified by an explicit rollback sub-eval, see § 6 E2). Quarantined-only artifacts (failed promotion) do NOT count toward Gate H. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:541:H5 (Gate H = at least one `Certified` artifact) is **NOT** a separate inferential test — it is a deterministic OR over per-artifact outcomes that already passed the family-corrected α (per § 5.4).

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:4203:Overall: **CHALLENGE**. The arc is not fundamentally broken, but the current PREREG has three preregistration-level ambiguities that peer review could exploit: heldout sealing is not actually sealed from agents, Gate H attribution is underdefined under a single heldout eval, and the statistical family/sample unit is not closed enough.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:4222:2. **Define Gate H measurement (§1.7, §6 E2-E3, §7 H):** specify per-seed aggregation, baseline/post protocol, per-artifact attribution, rollback test, and when `Certified` is minted, all within the “single sealed heldout eval” constraint.

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3:**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:26:**Mandate**: PRE-BATCH gate. The 576-run p_0 calibration is about to launch (~$3-5 API spend, ~8 wall-hours, output freezes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` AND becomes part of Trust Root). Any defect found BEFORE the batch is cheap; any defect found AFTER means recomputing the entire calibration (= retread the full $3-5 + 8h + Trust Root churn). PASS only if you would stake your independence on the batch result being valid.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:78:### Q3 — p_0 calibration semantics
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:80:PREREG § 5.5 specifies `p_0 = sum_p max_seed(regression_p) / 144` where `regression_p = 1 iff control SOLVED && treatment UNSOLVED`. Treatment short-circuits at tx 50.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:83:- (Q3.a) The synthetic short-circuit at tx 50 measures "how often does control take ≥ 50 tx to solve". Is that a FAIR proxy for "how often does a Phase E artifact corrupt mid-run state"? An artifact-induced failure at tx 50 in a real Phase E run would produce a different cost/time profile than our short-circuit. Does this matter for j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:84:- (Q3.b) `compute_p0.py` per-problem uses `max(over seeds)` — worst-case framing. If only 1 of 2 seeds regressed, the problem still counts as 1. With 2 seeds, this DOUBLES the regression rate vs `mean(over seeds)`. PREREG § 5.5 line 450 explicitly says max — agreed?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:85:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:86:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:87:- (Q3.e) Boltzmann seeds [31415, 2718] frozen in PREREG; `run_p0_calibration.sh` hardcodes them. If a future drift forces re-calibration, are the seeds in Trust Root manifest sufficient to detect tampering, or could a runner script tamper go undetected?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:104:- (Q5.a) Do these gaps affect p_0 calibration RESULT (not Phase D consumption)? `compute_p0.py` joins on calibration_problem_id + calibration_seed and reads only `solved` / `progress_verified` — neither field comes from WAL or recent_rejections. Argument for "no impact": the calibration jsonl IS ground-truth-validated independently. Verify.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:106:- (Q5.c) `failed_branch_count` and `total_run_token_count` for treatment-short-circuit rows are stamped at tx 50 (correctly reflects 50 tx of real activity). Does anything downstream INTERPRET these as "if they were lower than control, the treatment toggle is bug-prone"? compute_p0.py doesn't; flag if any other tooling does.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:110:PREREG § 5.5 ceiling: if measured p_0 > 0.10, ABORT. compute_p0.py exits 2 if ceiling violated.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:113:- (Q6.a) Is exit-2 actually an ABORT in the runner workflow, or could a downstream script swallow it and continue? Test by inspection: where in the pipeline does compute_p0.py run, and what handles exit code?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:114:- (Q6.b) p_0 > 0.10 means treatment failed too aggressively. The mitigation per PREREG = "redesign rollback simulation, redo". Are we confident the synthetic-short-circuit-at-tx-50 design is FAR enough from the ceiling that we won't hit it? If most adaptation problems take >50 tx to solve, p_0 will be HIGH. Pre-batch evidence (smoke) doesn't tell us this.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:115:- (Q6.c) If p_0 is in (0.05, 0.10] — borderline — is that a passive accept, or should a borderline result trigger automatic re-calibration with a different threshold (e.g., tx 75) to verify p_0 is stable?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:122:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:123:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:591:#                         (PREREG § 1.8). baseline_regression_rate +
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:593:#                         until B7-extra (p_0 calibration) lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:640:baseline_regression_rate = 0.0  # PLACEHOLDER until Phase B7-extra calibration
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:665:## handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh (NEW)
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:669:# PPUT-CCL B7-extra — p_0 calibration runner.

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1171:| `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/rollback_sim.rs` | B7-extra calibration toggle; PREREG § 5.5 commits a frozen `ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD = 50` and a binary `SIMULATE_ROLLBACK_AT_TX_50` env var — tampering with either defeats the p_0 measurement |
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1180:**Total manifest size**: 16 files as of 2026-04-25 (15 from B7 + `rollback_sim.rs` from B7-extra). Manifest size will grow by 1 more file when B7-extra calibration lands (the `experiments/minif2f_v4/logs/p0_calibration_*.jsonl` data file becomes part of Trust Root per PREREG § 5.5 freeze step).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1226:2. ~~**TRACE_MATRIX of B7-extra (p_0 calibration toggle)**~~ — landed. Final implementation differs slightly from the original sketch in this section: the constitutional `bus.register_predicate(...)` API does not currently exist on `main` (it lives on the unmerged `phase-z-wtool-tools` branch — TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC1-N11 references it aspirationally). Rather than scope-creep B7-extra into reviving Phase Z, the synthetic predicate is implemented at the evaluator layer in `rollback_sim.rs` with an explicit short-circuit at the threshold tx. The constitutional anchor (FC1-E18 ∏p=0 → Q_t repeated, then FC2-N22 HALT via existing `MaxTxExhausted`) is unchanged; only the abstraction depth differs. Listed under § 2 above as ✅ entries.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1270:2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1289:| `compute_p0.py` (PREREG § 5.5 estimator) | **None** — only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores cost. p_0 is unaffected. |
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1297:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1309:Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1311:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1358:- 📅 Phase E sealed eval on heldout-54 = ground-truth on capability **generalization** (Gate H requirement).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1385:- ✅ Trust Root + B7 Boot freeze + B7-extra p_0 calibration build the measurement scaffolding for this. North Star is achievable iff the chain above is honored.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1411:**Severity**: Phase D scope. Current implementation is acceptable for Phase B production runs (control); does not corrupt p_0 calibration (compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores recent_rejections content).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1422:- B7-extra (in flight): p_0 calibration via 576 runs

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:2922:406:For each artifact j ∈ {1, ..., k} (k ≤ k_max = 10) entering Phase E with `ArtifactState = Quarantined`, **three** primary inferential tests run on heldout-54 (NOT four — j-RR is a descriptive guardrail per § 5.4):
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:2923:437:### 5.5 p_0 calibration protocol

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:2931:462:Ceiling:       if measured p_0 > 0.10, ABORT — toggle too aggressive or system
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:2932:481:Gate H: count of artifacts j with ArtifactState(j) = Certified ≥ 1
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:2935:567:| H | **Heldout WBCG_PPUT > 0 with ArtifactState = Certified gate** (round 4 — j-RR moved out of inferential family per Codex round-3): at least one ArchitectAI-generated user-space artifact Δ has `ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified` per § 1.7 — meaning (i) was Accepted (passed § 3 + § 3.5 AuditorAI battery), (ii) was Quarantined (user-approved + ΔPPUT_meta_val > 0 + N_use ≥ 3 on meta_val), (iii) at heldout sealed eval **three** primary inferential tests reject null at family-corrected α (per § 5.3 + § 9): **j-PPUT** (sign test on n=54), **j-FAR** (non-inferiority sign test), **j-CPR** (non-inferiority sign test), AND (iv) **j-RR ≤ p_0 point check guardrail** (per § 5.4 — descriptive, NOT inferential, NO α correction), AND (v) is rollbackable (artifact directory deletion at the protocol level restores prior heldout PPUT — verified by an explicit rollback sub-eval, see § 6 E2). Quarantined-only artifacts (failed any of the five conditions) do NOT count toward Gate H. |

Task Progress remains binary (Lean perfect predicate). | Gemini DeepThink 2026-04-26 PPUT-driven FULL PASS confirmation |

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3029:   406	For each artifact j ∈ {1, ..., k} (k ≤ k_max = 10) entering Phase E with `ArtifactState = Quarantined`, **three** primary inferential tests run on heldout-54 (NOT four — j-RR is a descriptive guardrail per § 5.4):
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3037:   414	`j-RR` is **NOT** in the inferential family — see § 5.4.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3039:   416	### 5.4 j-RR as descriptive guardrail (round 4 fix per Codex round-3 R4-2)
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3041:   418	**Why this changed**: Round 3 placed j-RR in the inferential family with H0: `regression_rate ≥ p_0 = 0.10`. Codex round-3 noted this is mathematically unwinnable: best case 0/54 regressions gives `p = 0.9^54 ≈ 0.00343`, but smallest Holm threshold at N_max = 44 was 0.05/44 ≈ 0.00114 → fails to reject even with zero regressions. Gate H unreachable.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3043:   420	**Round 4 fix**: j-RR is reclassified as a **descriptive guardrail** (point estimate check, no α correction, no place in family):
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3046:   423	j-RR criterion (point check, NOT inferential):
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3055:   432	  Pass criterion: RR_j ≤ p_0   where p_0 is calibrated per § 5.5.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3058:   435	**Why descriptive is statistically correct here**: j-RR is a constraint, not a discovery hypothesis. We do not need to argue "RR_j is significantly less than p_0"; we only need RR_j ≤ p_0. A point check is precisely the right tool. No multiplicity correction applies because this is not a test.

Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3275:    56	| `compute_p0.py` (PREREG § 5.5 estimator) | **None** — only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores cost. p_0 is unaffected. |
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3283:    64	> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3295:    76	Thesis claim 7 (white-box predicates settle state) is satisfied for the **production path**: every real proposal flows through `bus.append → forbidden_patterns + Lean4Oracle` (white-box, deterministic, inspectable). The synthetic predicate's measurement-only path bypasses this for the specific purpose of estimating p_0 (a measurement quantity). The thesis applies to the system's claim about how it handles real proposals, not to how it does internal calibration measurements on itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3297:    78	Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3342:    28	- 📅 Phase E sealed eval on heldout-54 = ground-truth on capability **generalization** (Gate H requirement).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3369:    55	- ✅ Trust Root + B7 Boot freeze + B7-extra p_0 calibration build the measurement scaffolding for this. North Star is achievable iff the chain above is honored.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3395:    81	**Severity**: Phase D scope. Current implementation is acceptable for Phase B production runs (control); does not corrupt p_0 calibration (compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores recent_rejections content).

PASS only if you would stake your independence on the batch result being valid.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3757:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:40:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3761:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:77:- (Q3.b) `compute_p0.py` per-problem uses `max(over seeds)` — worst-case framing. If only 1 of 2 seeds regressed, the problem still counts as 1. With 2 seeds, this DOUBLES the regression rate vs `mean(over seeds)`. PREREG § 5.5 line 450 explicitly says max — agreed?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3762:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:78:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3763:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:79:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3764:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:80:- (Q3.e) Boltzmann seeds [31415, 2718] frozen in PREREG; `run_p0_calibration.sh` hardcodes them. If a future drift forces re-calibration, are the seeds in Trust Root manifest sufficient to detect tampering, or could a runner script tamper go undetected?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3765:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:97:- (Q5.a) Do these gaps affect p_0 calibration RESULT (not Phase D consumption)? `compute_p0.py` joins on calibration_problem_id + calibration_seed and reads only `solved` / `progress_verified` — neither field comes from WAL or recent_rejections. Argument for "no impact": the calibration jsonl IS ground-truth-validated independently. Verify.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3766:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:99:- (Q5.c) `failed_branch_count` and `total_run_token_count` for treatment-short-circuit rows are stamped at tx 50 (correctly reflects 50 tx of real activity). Does anything downstream INTERPRET these as "if they were lower than control, the treatment toggle is bug-prone"? compute_p0.py doesn't; flag if any other tooling does.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3767:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:103:PREREG § 5.5 ceiling: if measured p_0 > 0.10, ABORT. compute_p0.py exits 2 if ceiling violated.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3768:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:106:- (Q6.a) Is exit-2 actually an ABORT in the runner workflow, or could a downstream script swallow it and continue? Test by inspection: where in the pipeline does compute_p0.py run, and what handles exit code?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3769:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:116:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3771:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:166:printf '\n```\n\n## handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh (NEW)\n\n```bash\n' >> "$TMP_PROMPT"

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3773:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:169:printf '\n```\n\n## handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py (NEW)\n\n```python\n' >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3774:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:170:cat "${ROOT}/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py" >> "$TMP_PROMPT"
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3775:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:199:  printf '**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings\n'
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3778:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3:**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3780:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:19:    -   **Conclusion**: The equivalence is a convenience that is valid *only because* `compute_p0.py` ignores cost and intra-run state, joining only on the final `SOLVED`/`UNSOLVED` status. The constitutional anchor is weak; it achieves an outcome analogous to FC2-N22 HALT, but it does not *traverse* the constitutional path of FC1-E18 (repeatedly). The documentation in `rollback_sim.rs` should be amended to state this is a *functionally equivalent outcome for p_0 estimation* rather than a generally "observably equivalent" process.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3782:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:41:-   **(Q3.b) `max(over seeds)` framing**: **PASS**. `PREREG § 5.5` line 450 explicitly specifies `max over the 2 seeds`. The implementation in `compute_p0.py:84` correctly reflects this policy (`if regression > per_problem_regression[pid]: ...`). This is a consistent implementation of a pre-registered, "worst-case" policy choice.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3783:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:43:-   **(Q3.c) `solved()` predicate**: **PASS**. The `solved()` function in `compute_p0.py:44-48` correctly prioritizes `progress_verified` over the legacy `has_golden_path`, which aligns with the B4 audit findings and the `RunAggregate::V2` schema.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3784:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:45:-   **(Q3.d) Silent drop in `compute_p0.py`**: **CHALLENGE**. The script at `compute_p0.py:56-58` silently skips rows that are missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed`. A failure in the runner script (`run_p0_calibration.sh:170-177`) to stamp a row would cause that data point to be silently dropped, biasing the `p_0` result without warning. The script should fail loudly if any row from either input file is missing the required keys.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3785:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:-   **(Q3.e) Runner script seed tampering**: **CHALLENGE**. The seeds `[31415, 2718]` are frozen in `PREREG § 5.5` and hardcoded in `run_p0_calibration.sh:54`. However, the runner script itself is not in the Trust Root. An operator could modify the script to use different seeds, potentially gaming the `p_0` value, and this would not be detected by `boot::verify_trust_root`. While `PREREG.md` is hashed, this only allows for manual post-hoc detection of a discrepancy. This is a gap in automated verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3788:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:-   **(Q6.a) `exit 2` abort**: **CHALLENGE**. The runner script `run_p0_calibration.sh` does not use `set -e`. If `compute_p0.py` exits with code 2, the script will continue executing, printing the summary box. An automated runner could miss the failure. The script should use `set -e` or explicitly check the exit code of `compute_p0.py` and abort.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3789:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:71:-   **(Q7.b) Timeout impact on `p_0`**: **CHALLENGE**. A run that times out is logged as `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` (`run_p0_calibration.sh:195`). This means the (problem, seed) pair will be missing from one of the jsonl files. `compute_p0.py` will then exclude this pair from its analysis (`set(c.keys()) & set(t.keys())`). This introduces sampling bias. A timeout is a valid outcome (UNSOLVED) and should be treated as such, not as a data error. The runner script should be modified to emit a valid UNSOLVED jsonl row for timed-out runs.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3790:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:100:3.  **CHALLENGE Fix**: Modify `compute_p0.py` to fail loudly if any row is missing the required `calibration_*` keys, preventing silent data loss and biased results. (`Q3.d`)
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3791:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:101:4.  **CHALLENGE Fix**: Modify `run_p0_calibration.sh` to treat a timeout as a valid `UNSOLVED` outcome and emit a corresponding JSONL row, instead of a `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` that leads to data exclusion. (`Q7.b`)
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3798:./handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:518:                 `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3800:./handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:522:                 `experiments/minif2f_v4/logs/p0_calibration_2026-04-*.jsonl`
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3801:./handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:1327:                 `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3803:./handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:1331:                 `experiments/minif2f_v4/logs/p0_calibration_2026-04-*.jsonl`
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3806:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3:**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3807:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:26:**Mandate**: PRE-BATCH gate. The 576-run p_0 calibration is about to launch (~$3-5 API spend, ~8 wall-hours, output freezes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` AND becomes part of Trust Root). Any defect found BEFORE the batch is cheap; any defect found AFTER means recomputing the entire calibration (= retread the full $3-5 + 8h + Trust Root churn). PASS only if you would stake your independence on the batch result being valid.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3810:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3814:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:84:- (Q3.b) `compute_p0.py` per-problem uses `max(over seeds)` — worst-case framing. If only 1 of 2 seeds regressed, the problem still counts as 1. With 2 seeds, this DOUBLES the regression rate vs `mean(over seeds)`. PREREG § 5.5 line 450 explicitly says max — agreed?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3815:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:85:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3816:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:86:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3817:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:87:- (Q3.e) Boltzmann seeds [31415, 2718] frozen in PREREG; `run_p0_calibration.sh` hardcodes them. If a future drift forces re-calibration, are the seeds in Trust Root manifest sufficient to detect tampering, or could a runner script tamper go undetected?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3818:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:104:- (Q5.a) Do these gaps affect p_0 calibration RESULT (not Phase D consumption)? `compute_p0.py` joins on calibration_problem_id + calibration_seed and reads only `solved` / `progress_verified` — neither field comes from WAL or recent_rejections. Argument for "no impact": the calibration jsonl IS ground-truth-validated independently. Verify.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3819:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:106:- (Q5.c) `failed_branch_count` and `total_run_token_count` for treatment-short-circuit rows are stamped at tx 50 (correctly reflects 50 tx of real activity). Does anything downstream INTERPRET these as "if they were lower than control, the treatment toggle is bug-prone"? compute_p0.py doesn't; flag if any other tooling does.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3820:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:110:PREREG § 5.5 ceiling: if measured p_0 > 0.10, ABORT. compute_p0.py exits 2 if ceiling violated.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3821:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:113:- (Q6.a) Is exit-2 actually an ABORT in the runner workflow, or could a downstream script swallow it and continue? Test by inspection: where in the pipeline does compute_p0.py run, and what handles exit code?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3822:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:123:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3824:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:591:#                         (PREREG § 1.8). baseline_regression_rate +

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3853:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1180:**Total manifest size**: 16 files as of 2026-04-25 (15 from B7 + `rollback_sim.rs` from B7-extra). Manifest size will grow by 1 more file when B7-extra calibration lands (the `experiments/minif2f_v4/logs/p0_calibration_*.jsonl` data file becomes part of Trust Root per PREREG § 5.5 freeze step).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3855:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1270:2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3857:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1289:| `compute_p0.py` (PREREG § 5.5 estimator) | **None** — only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores cost. p_0 is unaffected. |
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3860:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1297:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3862:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1311:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3864:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1411:**Severity**: Phase D scope. Current implementation is acceptable for Phase B production runs (control); does not corrupt p_0 calibration (compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores recent_rejections content).

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4116:./handover/architect-insights/THESIS_V2_GROUND_TRUTH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:81:**Severity**: Phase D scope. Current implementation is acceptable for Phase B production runs (control); does not corrupt p_0 calibration (compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores recent_rejections content).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4118:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:37:2. **B7-extra refactor** (post-Phase Z merge): replace evaluator short-circuit with `bus.register_predicate(AlwaysRejectAfterTxN(ROLLBACK_TX_THRESHOLD))` at run_swarm boot when toggle is on. Drop the short-circuit. The synthetic_short_circuit flag becomes an artifact of how p_0 was historically calibrated; do not re-calibrate (Trust Root has the frozen value).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4120:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:56:| `compute_p0.py` (PREREG § 5.5 estimator) | **None** — only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED, ignores cost. p_0 is unaffected. |
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4123:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:64:> Crucially: when `synthetic_short_circuit == Some(true)`, the run's `total_run_token_count` (C_i) is **understated** vs a true 150-tx vetoed loop, because the LLM calls for tx 51-199 never happened. `compute_p0.py` ignores cost (only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED), so p_0 estimation is unaffected; downstream PPUT analysis on these rows MUST honor this flag and exclude or specially treat them.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4125:./handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md:78:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.

Manifest size will grow by 1 more file when B7-extra calibration lands (the `experiments/minif2f_v4/logs/p0_calibration_*.jsonl` data file becomes part of Trust Root per PREREG § 5.5 freeze step).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4227:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:21:**Mandate**: PRE-BATCH gate. The 576-run p_0 calibration is about to launch (~$3-5 API spend, ~8 wall-hours, output freezes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` AND becomes part of Trust Root). Any defect found BEFORE the batch is cheap; any defect found AFTER means recomputing the entire calibration. PASS only if you would stake your independence on the batch result being valid.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4239:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:227:          f"**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings\n"
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4240:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:19:**Mandate**: PRE-BATCH gate. The 576-run p_0 calibration is about to launch (~$3-5 API spend, ~8 wall-hours, output freezes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` AND becomes part of Trust Root). Any defect found BEFORE the batch is cheap; any defect found AFTER means recomputing the entire calibration (= retread the full $3-5 + 8h + Trust Root churn). PASS only if you would stake your independence on the batch result being valid.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4252:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:199:  printf '**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings\n'
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4264:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3:**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4268:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:-   **(Q3.e) Runner script seed tampering**: **CHALLENGE**. The seeds `[31415, 2718]` are frozen in `PREREG § 5.5` and hardcoded in `run_p0_calibration.sh:54`. However, the runner script itself is not in the Trust Root. An operator could modify the script to use different seeds, potentially gaming the `p_0` value, and this would not be detected by `boot::verify_trust_root`. While `PREREG.md` is hashed, this only allows for manual post-hoc detection of a discrepancy. This is a gap in automated verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4272:handover/audits/run_codex_pput_ccl_audit_round3.sh:44:- Round-3 R3-2: full protocol — toggle = `--simulate-rollback-at-tx-50`; sample = full adaptation-144 × 2 seeds = 288 paired runs; estimator = per-problem worst-case across seeds, p_0 = sum/144; schedule = Phase B7 mandatory (not deferrable to Phase D); freeze = genesis_payload.toml + Trust Root SHA-256 of jsonl; ceiling = p_0 > 0.10 ABORT (not auto-pass attack); audit = dual-audit at B → C transition checkpoint.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4281:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:518:                 `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4285:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:840:| 2026-04-26 (round 3) | **R3-2**: § 5.2 p_0 calibration protocol fully specified — toggle (`--simulate-rollback-at-tx-50`), sample (full adaptation-144 × 2 seeds = 288 paired runs), estimator (per-problem worst-case across seeds), schedule (Phase B7 mandatory before Phase D), freeze (genesis_payload.toml + Trust Root SHA-256 of calibration jsonl), ceiling (p_0 > 0.10 = ABORT), audit (dual-audit at Phase B → C transition). Closes round-2 tuning-surface concern. | Codex round-2 audit P0-gate-h (partial) |

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4310:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3:**Targets**: rollback_sim.rs + boot.rs + genesis_payload.toml + run_p0_calibration.sh + compute_p0.py + alignment + findings
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4311:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:26:**Mandate**: PRE-BATCH gate. The 576-run p_0 calibration is about to launch (~$3-5 API spend, ~8 wall-hours, output freezes into `genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate` AND becomes part of Trust Root). Any defect found BEFORE the batch is cheap; any defect found AFTER means recomputing the entire calibration (= retread the full $3-5 + 8h + Trust Root churn). PASS only if you would stake your independence on the batch result being valid.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4375:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1311:Thesis claim 11 (legible/enforceable/reversible) is satisfied: the synthetic_short_circuit flag makes the calibration disambiguator legible; the cost-asymmetry doc-comment makes it enforceable (any tool ignoring the flag is observably wrong); the calibration is reversible because it produces a frozen p_0 in genesis_payload.toml that can be re-measured if the toggle definition changes.
